toolUseResult.originalFile:
# Master Implementation Status - Neuman Brain Dashboard
**Created:** 2025-11-26
**Purpose:** Comprehensive audit of ALL features across user notes, research docs, and competitive analysis
**Status:** Draft - Awaiting User Approval
**Source Documents:**
- `tasks/Neuman Brain User Notes 11252025.txt` (User testing feedback)
- `tasks/research-document.md` (26 feature categories researched)
- `tasks/research-document-expanded-initial-dig.md` (50+ competitor analysis)

---

## Table of Contents
1. [Implementation Status Legend](#implementation-status-legend)
2. [Completed Features (100% Parity)](#completed-features-100-parity)
3. [Partial Implementation (<100% Parity)](#partial-implementation-100-parity)
4. [Not Started](#not-started)
5. [Research Needed](#research-needed)
6. [Foundation & Infrastructure](#foundation--infrastructure)
7. [Competitor Feature Matrix](#competitor-feature-matrix)

---

## Implementation Status Legend

- **COMPLETED (100%)** - Feature fully implemented with all desired functionality
- **PARTIAL (<100%)** - Feature exists but missing key capabilities or polish
- **NOT STARTED** - Feature identified but no implementation yet
- **RESEARCH NEEDED** - Requires investigation before implementation planning

---

## COMPLETED FEATURES (100% Parity)

### Dashboard & Widgets
✅ **Task Summary Widget** - Dynamically updates with Quick Add (Fixed 2025-11-25)
✅ **Upcoming Events Widget** - Height fixed, displays upcoming events (Fixed 2025-11-25)
✅ **Quick Add Task Button** - Color contrast fixed (Fixed 2025-11-25)
✅ **Widget Layout System** - Flexible dashboard widget grid

### Schedule & Calendar
✅ **ICS Calendar Import** - Date format fixed, now working (Fixed 2025-11-25)
✅ **Calendar "Today" Highlighting** - Current day clearly marked
✅ **Double-Click Day Behavior** - Opens DayDetailModal with events/tasks (Fixed 2025-11-25)
  - Shows all events for selected day
  - Shows all tasks due that day with navigation
  - "Add New Event" button
  - Goes straight to add if empty
✅ **Kanban Tasks on Calendar** - Tasks with due dates appear on calendar (Fixed 2025-11-25)
  - Blue styling to distinguish from events
  - Shows title, priority, status
  - Clickable in DayDetailModal
✅ **Monthly Calendar Unification** - Summary tab calendar matches Calendar tab style (Fixed 2025-11-26)
  - Added min-h-[80px] cells
  - Clickable days open detail modal
  - Shows time entries for selected day
✅ **Sidebar Navigation** - Expandable children for Schedule sections
✅ **Event Start Date Picker** - Events can be created for any date (Fixed 2025-11-26)
  - Editable start date field
  - Defaults to clicked calendar day
  - End date validation

### Notes
✅ **Folders Sidebar** - Button overlap fixed (Fixed 2025-11-25, Enhanced 2025-11-26)
  - Collapse button at right edge (no overlap)
  - Expand button when collapsed
  - Resize functionality with drag handle
  - Width persists 200-500px range
✅ **Notes CRUD** - Create, read, update, delete notes
✅ **Folder Organization** - Organize notes in folders
✅ **Daily Notes Pattern** - Obsidian/Roam-style daily journaling (Implemented 2025-11-26)
  - Keyboard shortcut (Ctrl/Cmd + D) to open today's note
  - Auto-create with customizable templates
  - Calendar view showing daily notes indicators
  - Settings UI: date format, template editor, folder selection
  - Auto-links to yesterday/tomorrow notes
  - Template variables: {date}, {yesterday}, {tomorrow}, {weekday}

### Settings
✅ **Display Name Autofill Prevention** - autoComplete="off" added (Fixed 2025-11-25)
✅ **Auto-Save Folder Display** - Shows configured folder correctly (Fixed 2025-11-25)
✅ **Backup Filename Format** - Shows "Neuman" with ".brain" outside (Fixed 2025-11-25)
✅ **Privacy Footer Cleanup** - Removed duplicate footer (Fixed 2025-11-25)

### Foundation & Data Integrity (2025-11-25)
✅ **Data Validation Layer** - src/services/validation.ts
  - Centralized Zod schemas
  - Date key format validation (YYYY-M-D)
  - Time format validation (HH:mm)
  - Legacy date normalization
✅ **Error Boundaries** - src/components/StoreErrorBoundary.tsx
  - Recovery UI with Try Again/Clear Data/Restore
  - Wrapped critical routes
✅ **Import Safety** - src/services/brainBackup.ts
  - Pre-import validation
  - Rollback snapshot
  - Automatic date normalization
  - Invalid data filtered

### UI Polish (2025-11-26)
✅ **Modal Overflow Fixes**
  - CardDetailPanel: Removed nested scroll
  - QuickAddModal: Added max-h-[85vh]
  - Proper content containment
✅ **MonthlyTimeReport Day Detail Modal**
  - Click any day to see entries
  - Shows project colors, descriptions, durations
  - "Add Time Entry" button

### AI & Terminal
✅ **AI Terminal Shell** - Basic shell emulation with error messaging
✅ **Error Messaging** - Clear explanations for unsupported commands

### Tasks & Project Management
✅ **Task CRUD** - Create, read, update, delete tasks
✅ **Task Due Dates** - Tasks can have due dates (start + due dates supported)
✅ **Task Priorities** - Priority levels (low/medium/high) with color coding
✅ **Task Status** - Status tracking with customizable columns

---

## PARTIAL IMPLEMENTATION (<100% Parity)

### Tasks & Project Management
🔶 **Kanban Board** (Status: PARTIAL - Reclassified 2025-11-28)
- **Audit Date:** 2025-11-28
- **Competitors Analyzed:** Trello, Asana Board View, Monday.com Kanban View
- **Current Features:**
  - ✅ Drag-and-drop between columns (@dnd-kit with pointer + touch sensors)
  - ✅ Customizable columns (ColumnManager, 3-7 column view options)
  - ✅ Card numbers (KAN-#) with search support - **UNIQUE ADVANTAGE**
  - ✅ Priority levels (low/medium/high) with color coding
  - ✅ Tags with autocomplete and filtering
  - ✅ Start dates + due dates
  - ✅ Checklists with real-time persistence
  - ✅ Comments with timestamps and activity log
  - ✅ Subtasks
  - ✅ Board and List views with toggle
  - ✅ Search (by title, description, or card number)
  - ✅ Advanced filters (priority, tags) with collapsible panel
  - ✅ Archived tasks view
  - ✅ Quick Add modal (Cmd/Ctrl+K global shortcut)
  - ✅ Keyboard navigation (arrow keys, Enter, Delete, ? for help)
  - ✅ Undo/Redo with toast notifications - **UNIQUE ADVANTAGE**
  - ✅ Auto-save on blur
  - ✅ View mode persistence (localStorage)
  - ✅ Effort estimates (XS/S/M/L/XL)
  - ✅ Custom status support
  - ✅ Time estimates and tracking integration
  - ✅ Custom fields integration
  - ✅ Recurring tasks integration
  - ✅ Task dependencies (blocks/blocked-by relationships)
- **Critical Gaps (Preventing 100%+ Parity):**
  1. ❌ **Assignees/Members** - Cannot assign tasks to team members
     - Trello: ✅ Multiple assignees per card
     - Asana: ✅ Single assignee + followers
     - Monday.com: ✅ Multiple people per task
     - **Impact:** MAJOR blocker for team collaboration - baseline feature in ALL competitors
  2. ❌ **File Attachments** - Cannot attach files to cards
     - Trello: ✅ Drag-drop images/docs, cloud storage integration
     - Asana: ✅ File attachments with preview
     - Monday.com: ✅ File columns with covers (crop/fit modes)
     - **Impact:** Limits use case to simple text-only tasks - baseline in ALL competitors
  3. ❌ **WIP Limits** - Cannot set column task limits
     - Monday.com: ✅ Task limits per column with warnings
     - Trello: Available via Power-Up
     - **Impact:** Missing key Kanban methodology feature
  4. ❌ **Card Covers/Images** - No visual covers on cards
     - Trello: ✅ Image covers from attachments
     - Monday.com: ✅ File column covers (crop/fit modes)
     - **Impact:** Less visual, harder to scan board at a glance
  5. ⚠️ **Calendar View Integration** - Calendar exists but on separate page
     - Trello: Premium feature, integrated view
     - Asana: ✅ One-click view switch
     - Monday.com: ✅ Instant view toggle
     - **Impact:** Less convenient workflow, requires navigation
- **Our Unique Advantages:**
  - ✅ Card numbers (KAN-#) - Unique to us, professional tracking
  - ✅ Undo/Redo - Unique to us, prevents mistakes
  - ✅ Better keyboard shortcuts than competitors
  - ✅ Offline-first, local storage (privacy advantage)
  - ✅ No paywalls (Timeline/Gantt included for free)
  - ✅ Faster performance (60KB vs 2-4MB competitors)
- **Files:** `src/widgets/Kanban.tsx`, `src/widgets/Kanban/CardDetailPanel.tsx`, `src/widgets/Kanban/KanbanCard.tsx`, `src/widgets/Kanban/KanbanColumn.tsx`, `src/widgets/Kanban/ListView.tsx`
- **Priority:** **P0** (Assignees, File Attachments), **P1** (WIP Limits, Card Covers)
- **Verdict:** MVP-level Kanban with excellent offline/keyboard UX, but missing baseline team collaboration features
- **Sources:** [Trello Kanban Features](https://www.projectmanager.com/blog/trello-kanban-board), [Asana Board View](https://help.asana.com/s/article/board-view?language=en_US), [Monday.com Kanban](https://support.monday.com/hc/en-us/articles/360000661379-The-Kanban-View)

🔶 **Timeline/Gantt View** (Status: PARTIAL - Reclassified 2025-11-28)
- **Audit Date:** 2025-11-28
- **Competitors Analyzed:** Monday.com Gantt Chart, Asana Gantt View, Microsoft Project
- **Current Features:**
  - ✅ Task bars with start/due dates visualization
  - ✅ Drag bars to reschedule (updates startDate/dueDate in real-time)
  - ✅ Resize bars to change duration
  - ✅ 3 zoom levels (day/week/month) with responsive scale
  - ✅ Today marker with indicator dot and label
  - ✅ Swimlanes by task status (backlog/todo/inprogress/review/done) - **UNIQUE ADVANTAGE**
  - ✅ Priority-based color coding
  - ✅ Dependency arrows (SimpleTaskDependency with SVG overlay)
  - ✅ Row-based positioning for clarity
  - ✅ Responsive horizontal scrolling
  - ✅ Empty state handling with helpful messaging
  - ✅ Tab interface in Tasks page (seamless Board ↔ Timeline toggle)
- **Critical Gaps (Preventing 100%+ Parity):**
  1. ❌ **Auto-shift Dependent Tasks** - When parent task moves, dependent tasks don't auto-adjust
     - Monday.com: ✅ "Shift all dependent tasks by however many days needed"
     - Asana: ✅ "Auto-scheduling" feature - blocked tasks automatically adjust
     - MS Project: ✅ Standard behavior (core feature)
     - **Impact:** CRITICAL - Manual rescheduling defeats entire purpose of dependencies. Baseline in ALL 3 professional tools.
  2. ❌ **Critical Path Analysis** - Cannot identify project bottlenecks
     - Monday.com: ✅ Critical path visualization with highlighting
     - Asana: ✅ Premium+ feature showing longest task sequence
     - MS Project: ✅ Core feature with critical task highlighting
     - **Impact:** Cannot optimize project timeline or identify delays
  3. ❌ **Baseline Comparison** - Cannot track planned vs actual schedule
     - Monday.com: ✅ Baseline snapshot with green/red variance indicators, formula column for day difference
     - MS Project: ✅ Up to 10 baselines with comprehensive variance tracking
     - Asana: ❌ Not available
     - **Impact:** No way to measure schedule variance or project health
  4. ❌ **Progress Tracking (% Complete)** - No visual completion indicators on bars
     - Monday.com: ✅ Shows progress fill within bars
     - Asana: ✅ Task percentage of completion visible
     - MS Project: ✅ Progress bars within Gantt bars (standard)
     - **Impact:** Can't see task progress at a glance, must open each task
  5. ❌ **Milestone Markers** - No way to mark key deadlines
     - Monday.com: ✅ Milestone tracking with special markers
     - Asana: ✅ Milestones as special task type with diamond marker
     - MS Project: ✅ Diamond milestone markers (standard)
     - **Impact:** Can't highlight critical dates or project gates
  6. ❌ **Subitems on Timeline** - Subtasks don't appear on timeline
     - Monday.com: ✅ Subitems supported in Gantt
     - MS Project: ✅ Subtask hierarchy on timeline
     - **Impact:** Can't see task breakdown in timeline view
  7. ❌ **Export Timeline** - Cannot export to PDF/Excel
     - Monday.com: ✅ Export up to 3-6 years, 640 items to Excel
     - MS Project: ✅ Export to PDF/Excel/multiple formats
     - **Impact:** Can't share timeline with stakeholders outside app
- **Our Unique Advantages:**
  - ✅ Swimlanes by status (clearer organization than Asana)
  - ✅ Integrated with Kanban in single page (seamless workflow)
  - ✅ Free (Asana Gantt is Premium+ only)
  - ✅ Offline-first (works without internet)
  - ✅ Fast performance
- **Files:** `src/components/GanttView/GanttTimeline.tsx` (lines 1-215), `src/components/GanttView/TimelineScale.tsx`, `src/components/GanttView/TaskBar.tsx`, `src/components/GanttView/DependencyLine.tsx`
- **Priority:** **P0** (Auto-shift dependencies, Critical path, Baseline tracking), **P1** (Progress %, Milestones), **P2** (Export, Subitems)
- **Verdict:** MVP-level Timeline with good basic visualization, but missing ALL professional project management features
- **Sources:** [Monday.com Gantt](https://support.monday.com/hc/en-us/articles/360015643840-The-Gantt-Chart-View-and-Widget), [Asana Gantt](https://help.asana.com/s/article/gantt-view?language=en_US), [MS Project Gantt](https://www.projectmanager.com/blog/microsoft-project-gantt-chart)

## FOUNDATION & INFRASTRUCTURE

### Current Foundation (✅ Completed)
- **Tech Stack:** React 19, TypeScript 5.9, Vite 7, Zustand 5, Tailwind 3, IndexedDB
- **Architecture:** Local-first, zero-cloud by default, privacy-first
- **Data Layer:** IndexedDB with Zustand state management
- **Validation:** Zod schemas for data integrity
- **Error Handling:** Error boundaries, recovery UI
- **Import/Export:** .brain format, ICS import, CSV export (planned)
- **Performance:** 60KB bundle size (vs 2-4MB competitors)
- **Offline:** Full offline functionality

### Infrastructure Gaps
⬜ **End-to-End Encryption for Sync**
- **Status:** Planned 2026
- **Implementation:** WebCrypto API, user keys
- **Priority:** HIGH (privacy differentiator)

⬜ **Service Worker for Offline Automation**
- **Use Case:** Run automations without browser open
- **Examples:** Daily task creation, reminders
- **Implementation:** Service Worker + Background Sync API
- **Priority:** HIGH (for automation features)

⬜ **IndexedDB Query Optimization**
- **Current:** Basic IndexedDB usage
- **Needed:** Compound indexes, full-text search
- **Priority:** MEDIUM

⬜ **Plugin System**
- **Inspiration:** Obsidian plugins
- **Use Case:** User-created extensions
- **Implementation:** Sandboxed JavaScript API
- **Priority:** LOW (future)

⬜ **Theme System**
- **Current:** Single theme
- **Needed:** Multiple themes, user-created
- **Priority:** LOW

⬜ **Export Formats**
- **Current:** .brain backup
- **Needed:** JSON, Markdown, CSV, PDF
- **Priority:** MEDIUM

⬜ **Import From Competitors**
- **Planned:** Trello, Asana, Todoist
- **Status:** Some work started
- **Priority:** HIGH (acquisition funnel)

---

## MARKET POSITIONING

### Neuman Brain's Blue Ocean Opportunity
**No competitor combines:**
1. ✅ Professional project management tools
2. ⬜ Personal habit tracking + gamification
3. ✅ Local-first privacy (no forced login)
4. ⬜ Offline automation
5. ✅ 100% free (no paywalls)
6. ✅ Fast (60KB vs 2-4MB)

### Cost Advantage
| Competitor | Annual Cost (10 users) |
|------------|------------------------|
| Monday.com | $1,440 - $2,400 |
| ClickUp | $1,080 - $2,280 |
| Asana | $1,620 - $3,660 |
| Todoist | $480 - $840 |
| Toggl Track | $1,080 - $2,160 |
| **Neuman Brain** | **$0** |

### Speed Advantage
- **NB:** 60KB bundle, <1s load time
- **Competitors:** 2-4MB, 3-10s load time
- **Result:** 10x-40x faster

### Privacy Advantage
- **NB:** Local-first by default, no tracking, optional E2EE sync
- **Competitors:** Cloud-only, data on their servers, telemetry

## PRIORITIZATION FRAMEWORK

### P0 - CRITICAL (Now)
1. ✅ All P0 bugs fixed (per user testing notes)
2. ✅ Notes polish research + implementation (COMPLETE - 5 Quick Wins implemented in 4 hours)
3. ✅ Time tracking dedicated section (ALREADY IMPLEMENTED - /schedule page with 5 tabs)
4. ✅ Browser bookmark sync research (Implementation pending user approval)

### P1 - HIGH (Next 3 months) ✅ 100% COMPLETE! 🎉
1. ✅ Recurring tasks (COMPLETE - 2025-11-26)
2. ✅ Task automation engine (MVP COMPLETE - 2025-11-26)
3. ✅ My Day dashboard (COMPLETE - 2025-11-26)
4. ✅ Import from competitors (COMPLETE - 2025-11-26) - Trello, Asana, Todoist, ClickUp, Monday, Notion
5. ✅ Backlinks/wikilinks for notes (COMPLETE - 2025-11-26) - Integration only, infrastructure already existed
6. ✅ Enhanced time tracking (COMPLETE - 2025-11-26) - MVP with billable hours & revenue analytics
   - ✅ Billable hours tracking UI
   - ✅ Revenue analytics card in monthly summary
   - ✅ Billable indicators in entry list
   - ✅ Real-time revenue calculations
   - ✅ CSV export (already existed)

**ALL P1 PRIORITIES SHIPPED!** 6/6 complete in single session.

### P2 - MEDIUM (3-6 months)
1. ✅ Gantt/Timeline view (COMPLETE - 2025-11-26) - Professional timeline visualization
   - ✅ Timeline view with day/week/month zoom levels
   - ✅ Drag task bars to reschedule (updates startDate/dueDate)
   - ✅ Resize bars to change duration
   - ✅ Dependency arrows connecting related tasks
   - ✅ Swimlanes grouped by task status
   - ✅ Priority-based color coding
   - ✅ Today marker indicator
   - ✅ Tab interface in Tasks page
   - Time: ~2 hours (6 hours under estimate)
2. ✅ Task dependencies (DISCOVERED COMPLETE - existed in CardDetailPanel)
   - ✅ Blocks/blocked-by relationship management
   - ✅ Full UI in CardDetailPanel (lines 559-706)
   - ✅ SimpleTaskDependency system
3. ✅ Custom fields (COMPLETE - 2025-11-26) - User-defined metadata fields
   - ✅ Field definitions for tasks and notes (text/number/date/select/checkbox)
   - ✅ CRUD UI in Settings page (src/components/CustomFieldsSettings.tsx)
   - ✅ Type-aware field editor with validation (src/components/CustomFieldEditor.tsx)
   - ✅ Integration in CardDetailPanel and NotesEditor
   - ✅ Optional fields (backward compatible)
   - Time: ~3.2 hours
4. ✅ Tags system for notes (COMPLETE - 2025-11-26) - Comprehensive tag management
   - ✅ Tag picker with autocomplete (src/components/TagPicker.tsx)
   - ✅ Tag filter sidebar with AND logic (src/components/TagFilter.tsx)
   - ✅ Tag manager modal with rename/delete (src/components/TagManager.tsx)
   - ✅ Global tag operations (rename/delete across all notes)
   - ✅ Tag statistics and recent tags
   - ✅ Custom hooks (useTags, useTagCounts, useNotesWithTags, useRecentTags)
   - ✅ Integrated into Notes page sidebar
   - Time: ~2.5 hours
5. ✅ Daily notes pattern (COMPLETE - 2025-11-26) - Obsidian/Roam-style daily journaling
   - ✅ Auto-create daily notes with keyboard shortcut (Ctrl/Cmd + D)
   - ✅ Customizable templates with date variables
   - ✅ Calendar view with daily notes indicators
   - ✅ Full settings UI (date format, template editor, folder selection)
   - ✅ Auto-linking to yesterday/tomorrow notes
6. ✅ Graph view (Phase 1 complete 2025-11-26)
   - ✅ Interactive D3.js force-directed graph visualization
   - ✅ Nodes for notes (cyan) and tags (magenta)
   - ✅ Edges from linkedNotes (backlinks)
   - ✅ Zoom, pan, and drag node interactions
   - ✅ Search filter and hide orphans toggle
   - ✅ Click nodes to navigate to notes
   - ✅ Dark mode compatible with semantic tokens
   - 🔶 Phase 2: Enhanced controls (tag/folder filters), tooltips, legend
7. ⬜ Diagram/whiteboard widget
8. ⬜ Form builder
9. ⬜ Personal analytics dashboard

### P3 - LOW (6-12 months)

2. ⬜ Database widget

### P4 - FUTURE (12+ months)

5. ⬜ Advanced automation

## IMPLEMENTATION RECOMMENDATIONS

### Immediate Next Steps
1. **Research Notes Competitors** - Understand what makes notes feel "polished"
2. **Design Time Tracking Section** - Create mockup for Schedule page integration
3. **Browser Extension Feasibility** - Research bookmark sync approach
4. **Automation Engine Architecture** - Plan Service Worker + IndexedDB system

### Quick Wins (Low Effort, High Impact)
1. ⬜ Tags for notes (hashtag syntax)
2. ⬜ Note templates (basic version)
3. ⬜ Task completion celebrations (animations)
4. ⬜ Natural language task dates
5. ⬜ Export to Markdown

### Technical Debt to Address
1. ⬜ IndexedDB query optimization (compound indexes)
2. ⬜ Full-text search implementation
3. ⬜ Service Worker setup for offline automation
4. ⬜ Bundle size analysis and optimization

### Architecture Decisions Needed
1. **Block-based editor?** - Research Notion/Obsidian approaches
2. **Graph database for notes?** - For backlinks/graph view
3. **Local AI models?** - Feasibility for on-device processing
4. **CRDT library?** - For future collaboration

---

## SUCCESS METRICS

### User Acquisition
- **Target:** Users switching from paid competitors
- **Funnel:** Import tools from Monday, ClickUp, Asana, Trello, Todoist

### Feature Parity
- **Goal:** Match 80% of Asana/Notion features (core 20% use cases)
- **Current:** ~40% estimated
- **Timeline:** 80% by Q4 2026

### Performance
- **Goal:** Maintain <100KB bundle size
- **Current:** 60KB ✅
- **Goal:** <1s load time (95th percentile)
- **Goal:** <100ms interaction latency

### Privacy
- **Goal:** Zero telemetry by default
- **Current:** ✅ Achieved
- **Goal:** E2EE sync by Q2 2026

- 50,000+ word analysis

input.new_string:
- **Sources:** [Monday.com Gantt](https://support.monday.com/hc/en-us/articles/360015643840-The-Gantt-Chart-View-and-Widget), [Asana Gantt](https://help.asana.com/s/article/gantt-view?language=en_US), [MS Project Gantt](https://www.projectmanager.com/blog/microsoft-project-gantt-chart)

🔶 **Recurring Tasks** (Status: PARTIAL - Reclassified 2025-11-28)
- **Audit Date:** 2025-11-28
- **Competitors Analyzed:** Todoist, ClickUp, Asana
- **Current Features:**
  - ✅ Frequency patterns: daily/weekly/monthly/yearly
  - ✅ Custom intervals (every 2 weeks, every 3 months, etc.)
  - ✅ Days of week selection (for weekly recurrence)
  - ✅ Day of month selection (for monthly recurrence, 1-31)
  - ✅ End conditions: never/after N occurrences/until date
  - ✅ On-demand instance generation (not pre-generated)
  - ✅ Parent/instance relationship tracking (recurrenceId, isRecurringParent)
  - ✅ Next occurrence calculation
  - ✅ Instance deduplication (prevents duplicate instances)
  - ✅ Series end detection (respects endCount and endDate)
  - ✅ RecurrencePicker UI component integrated into CardDetailPanel
  - ✅ Visual recurrence editor (matches Calendar Events pattern)
  - ✅ Full RRULE-like pattern support
- **Critical Gaps (Preventing 100%+ Parity):**
  1. ❌ **Recur from Completion Date** - Tasks always recur from original date, not completion date
     - Todoist: ✅ "every" vs "every!" syntax - "every! 3 months" recurs 3 months from completion
     - Asana: ✅ "Periodically" option - repeats 1-30 days after task is completed
     - ClickUp: ✅ "Recur only after completion" checkbox
     - **Impact:** CRITICAL for maintenance tasks, habits, and periodic reviews where completion date matters
     - **Example:** "Change oil filter every 3 months" should be 3 months from LAST change, not original date
  2. ❌ **Natural Language Input** - No "every Monday" or "monthly" parsing
     - Todoist: ✅ Full natural language parsing ("every third Thursday", "monthly", "every 2 weeks on Friday")
     - ClickUp: Partial (date picker focused)
     - Asana: ❌ No natural language
     - **Impact:** Slower task creation, requires clicking through UI
  3. ❌ **Template Integration** - Recurring tasks don't use task templates
     - ClickUp: ✅ Recurring tasks can use task templates - each instance starts with same structure/checklists
     - Asana: ⚠️ Subtasks copy but not full templates
     - Todoist: ❌ No templates
     - **Impact:** Each recurring instance is identical copy - can't have dynamic checklists or varying structure
  4. ⚠️ **Recurrence Exceptions** - Can edit instances but no visual indication
     - Have `recurrenceException` field in types but not fully implemented in UI
     - No "edit just this instance" vs "edit all future instances" workflow
     - **Impact:** Confusing UX when instances are modified
- **Our Unique Advantages:**
  - ✅ On-demand generation (doesn't clutter task list with future instances)
  - ✅ Matches Calendar Events pattern (consistent UX)
  - ✅ Offline-first (works without internet)
  - ✅ Clean parent/instance relationship model
- **Files:** `src/services/taskRecurrence.ts` (lines 1-201), `src/utils/recurrence.ts`, `src/components/RecurrencePicker.tsx`, `src/types/index.ts` (lines 209-221)
- **Priority:** **P0** (Recur from completion), **P1** (Natural language, Template integration), **P2** (Exception handling UI)
- **Verdict:** Strong recurrence engine with professional patterns, but missing key flexibility (completion-based recurrence) that users expect
- **Sources:** [Todoist Recurring Tasks](https://www.todoist.com/help/articles/introduction-to-recurring-dates-YUYVJJAV), [ClickUp Recurring Tasks](https://help.clickup.com/hc/en-us/articles/6309885016471-Use-recurring-tasks), [Asana Recurring Tasks](https://blog.coupler.io/asana-recurring-tasks/)

### Dashboard Widgets

  - ✅ Day of month selection (for monthly recurrence, 1-31)
  - ✅ End conditions: never/after N occurrences/until date
  - ✅ On-demand instance generation (not pre-generated)
  - ✅ Parent/instance relationship tracking (recurrenceId, isRecurringParent)
  - ✅ Next occurrence calculation
  - ✅ Instance deduplication (prevents duplicate instances)
  - ✅ Series end detection (respects endCount and endDate)
  - ✅ RecurrencePicker UI component integrated into CardDetailPanel
  - ✅ Visual recurrence editor (matches Calendar Events pattern)
  - ✅ Full RRULE-like pattern support
- **Critical Gaps (Preventing 100%+ Parity):**
  1. ❌ **Recur from Completion Date** - Tasks always recur from original date, not completion date
     - Todoist: ✅ "every" vs "every!" syntax - "every! 3 months" recurs 3 months from completion
     - Asana: ✅ "Periodically" option - repeats 1-30 days after task is completed
     - ClickUp: ✅ "Recur only after completion" checkbox
     - **Impact:** CRITICAL for maintenance tasks, habits, and periodic reviews where completion date matters
     - **Example:** "Change oil filter every 3 months" should be 3 months from LAST change, not original date
  2. ❌ **Natural Language Input** - No "every Monday" or "monthly" parsing
     - Todoist: ✅ Full natural language parsing ("every third Thursday", "monthly", "every 2 weeks on Friday")
     - ClickUp: Partial (date picker focused)
     - Asana: ❌ No natural language
     - **Impact:** Slower task creation, requires clicking through UI
  3. ❌ **Template Integration** - Recurring tasks don't use task templates
     - ClickUp: ✅ Recurring tasks can use task templates - each instance starts with same structure/checklists
     - Asana: ⚠️ Subtasks copy but not full templates
     - Todoist: ❌ No templates
     - **Impact:** Each recurring instance is identical copy - can't have dynamic checklists or varying structure
  4. ⚠️ **Recurrence Exceptions** - Can edit instances but no visual indication
     - Have `recurrenceException` field in types but not fully implemented in UI
     - No "edit just this instance" vs "edit all future instances" workflow
     - **Impact:** Confusing UX when instances are modified
- **Our Unique Advantages:**
  - ✅ On-demand generation (doesn't clutter task list with future instances)
  - ✅ Matches Calendar Events pattern (consistent UX)
  - ✅ Offline-first (works without internet)
  - ✅ Clean parent/instance relationship model
- **Files:** `src/services/taskRecurrence.ts` (lines 1-201), `src/utils/recurrence.ts`, `src/components/RecurrencePicker.tsx`, `src/types/index.ts` (lines 209-221)
- **Priority:** **P0** (Recur from completion), **P1** (Natural language, Template integration), **P2** (Exception handling UI)
- **Verdict:** Strong recurrence engine with professional patterns, but missing key flexibility (completion-based recurrence) that users expect
- **Sources:** [Todoist Recurring Tasks](https://www.todoist.com/help/articles/introduction-to-recurring-dates-YUYVJJAV), [ClickUp Recurring Tasks](https://help.clickup.com/hc/en-us/articles/6309885016471-Use-recurring-tasks), [Asana Recurring Tasks](https://blog.coupler.io/asana-recurring-tasks/)

### Dashboard Widgets

## PARTIAL IMPLEMENTATION (<100% Parity)

### Tasks & Project Management
🔶 **Kanban Board** (Status: PARTIAL - Reclassified 2025-11-28)
- **Audit Date:** 2025-11-28
- **Competitors Analyzed:** Trello, Asana Board View, Monday.com Kanban View
- **Current Features:**
  - ✅ Drag-and-drop between columns (@dnd-kit with pointer + touch sensors)
  - ✅ Customizable columns (ColumnManager, 3-7 column view options)
  - ✅ Card numbers (KAN-#) with search support - **UNIQUE ADVANTAGE**
  - ✅ Priority levels (low/medium/high) with color coding
  - ✅ Tags with autocomplete and filtering
  - ✅ Start dates + due dates
  - ✅ Checklists with real-time persistence
  - ✅ Comments with timestamps and activity log
  - ✅ Subtasks
  - ✅ Board and List views with toggle
  - ✅ Search (by title, description, or card number)
  - ✅ Advanced filters (priority, tags) with collapsible panel
  - ✅ Archived tasks view
  - ✅ Quick Add modal (Cmd/Ctrl+K global shortcut)
  - ✅ Keyboard navigation (arrow keys, Enter, Delete, ? for help)
  - ✅ Undo/Redo with toast notifications - **UNIQUE ADVANTAGE**
  - ✅ Auto-save on blur
  - ✅ View mode persistence (localStorage)
  - ✅ Effort estimates (XS/S/M/L/XL)
  - ✅ Custom status support
  - ✅ Time estimates and tracking integration
  - ✅ Custom fields integration
  - ✅ Recurring tasks integration
  - ✅ Task dependencies (blocks/blocked-by relationships)
- **Critical Gaps (Preventing 100%+ Parity):**
  1. ❌ **Assignees/Members** - Cannot assign tasks to team members
     - Trello: ✅ Multiple assignees per card
     - Asana: ✅ Single assignee + followers
     - Monday.com: ✅ Multiple people per task
     - **Impact:** MAJOR blocker for team collaboration - baseline feature in ALL competitors
  2. ❌ **File Attachments** - Cannot attach files to cards
     - Trello: ✅ Drag-drop images/docs, cloud storage integration
     - Asana: ✅ File attachments with preview
     - Monday.com: ✅ File columns with covers (crop/fit modes)
     - **Impact:** Limits use case to simple text-only tasks - baseline in ALL competitors
  3. ❌ **WIP Limits** - Cannot set column task limits
     - Monday.com: ✅ Task limits per column with warnings
     - Trello: Available via Power-Up
     - **Impact:** Missing key Kanban methodology feature
  4. ❌ **Card Covers/Images** - No visual covers on cards
     - Trello: ✅ Image covers from attachments
     - Monday.com: ✅ File column covers (crop/fit modes)
     - **Impact:** Less visual, harder to scan board at a glance
  5. ⚠️ **Calendar View Integration** - Calendar exists but on separate page
     - Trello: Premium feature, integrated view
     - Asana: ✅ One-click view switch
     - Monday.com: ✅ Instant view toggle
     - **Impact:** Less convenient workflow, requires navigation
- **Our Unique Advantages:**
  - ✅ Card numbers (KAN-#) - Unique to us, professional tracking
  - ✅ Undo/Redo - Unique to us, prevents mistakes
  - ✅ Better keyboard shortcuts than competitors
  - ✅ Offline-first, local storage (privacy advantage)
  - ✅ No paywalls (Timeline/Gantt included for free)
  - ✅ Faster performance (60KB vs 2-4MB competitors)
- **Files:** `src/widgets/Kanban.tsx`, `src/widgets/Kanban/CardDetailPanel.tsx`, `src/widgets/Kanban/KanbanCard.tsx`, `src/widgets/Kanban/KanbanColumn.tsx`, `src/widgets/Kanban/ListView.tsx`
- **Priority:** **P0** (Assignees, File Attachments), **P1** (WIP Limits, Card Covers)
- **Verdict:** MVP-level Kanban with excellent offline/keyboard UX, but missing baseline team collaboration features
- **Sources:** [Trello Kanban Features](https://www.projectmanager.com/blog/trello-kanban-board), [Asana Board View](https://help.asana.com/s/article/board-view?language=en_US), [Monday.com Kanban](https://support.monday.com/hc/en-us/articles/360000661379-The-Kanban-View)

🔶 **Timeline/Gantt View** (Status: PARTIAL - Reclassified 2025-11-28)
- **Audit Date:** 2025-11-28
- **Competitors Analyzed:** Monday.com Gantt Chart, Asana Gantt View, Microsoft Project
- **Current Features:**
  - ✅ Task bars with start/due dates visualization
  - ✅ Drag bars to reschedule (updates startDate/dueDate in real-time)
  - ✅ Resize bars to change duration
  - ✅ 3 zoom levels (day/week/month) with responsive scale
  - ✅ Today marker with indicator dot and label
  - ✅ Swimlanes by task status (backlog/todo/inprogress/review/done) - **UNIQUE ADVANTAGE**
  - ✅ Priority-based color coding
  - ✅ Dependency arrows (SimpleTaskDependency with SVG overlay)
  - ✅ Row-based positioning for clarity
  - ✅ Responsive horizontal scrolling
  - ✅ Empty state handling with helpful messaging
  - ✅ Tab interface in Tasks page (seamless Board ↔ Timeline toggle)
- **Critical Gaps (Preventing 100%+ Parity):**
  1. ❌ **Auto-shift Dependent Tasks** - When parent task moves, dependent tasks don't auto-adjust
     - Monday.com: ✅ "Shift all dependent tasks by however many days needed"
     - Asana: ✅ "Auto-scheduling" feature - blocked tasks automatically adjust
     - MS Project: ✅ Standard behavior (core feature)
     - **Impact:** CRITICAL - Manual rescheduling defeats entire purpose of dependencies. Baseline in ALL 3 professional tools.
  2. ❌ **Critical Path Analysis** - Cannot identify project bottlenecks
     - Monday.com: ✅ Critical path visualization with highlighting
     - Asana: ✅ Premium+ feature showing longest task sequence
     - MS Project: ✅ Core feature with critical task highlighting
     - **Impact:** Cannot optimize project timeline or identify delays
  3. ❌ **Baseline Comparison** - Cannot track planned vs actual schedule
     - Monday.com: ✅ Baseline snapshot with green/red variance indicators, formula column for day difference
     - MS Project: ✅ Up to 10 baselines with comprehensive variance tracking
     - Asana: ❌ Not available
     - **Impact:** No way to measure schedule variance or project health
  4. ❌ **Progress Tracking (% Complete)** - No visual completion indicators on bars
     - Monday.com: ✅ Shows progress fill within bars
     - Asana: ✅ Task percentage of completion visible
     - MS Project: ✅ Progress bars within Gantt bars (standard)
     - **Impact:** Can't see task progress at a glance, must open each task
  5. ❌ **Milestone Markers** - No way to mark key deadlines
     - Monday.com: ✅ Milestone tracking with special markers
     - Asana: ✅ Milestones as special task type with diamond marker
     - MS Project: ✅ Diamond milestone markers (standard)
     - **Impact:** Can't highlight critical dates or project gates
  6. ❌ **Subitems on Timeline** - Subtasks don't appear on timeline
     - Monday.com: ✅ Subitems supported in Gantt
     - MS Project: ✅ Subtask hierarchy on timeline
     - **Impact:** Can't see task breakdown in timeline view
  7. ❌ **Export Timeline** - Cannot export to PDF/Excel
     - Monday.com: ✅ Export up to 3-6 years, 640 items to Excel
     - MS Project: ✅ Export to PDF/Excel/multiple formats
     - **Impact:** Can't share timeline with stakeholders outside app
- **Our Unique Advantages:**
  - ✅ Swimlanes by status (clearer organization than Asana)
  - ✅ Integrated with Kanban in single page (seamless workflow)
  - ✅ Free (Asana Gantt is Premium+ only)
  - ✅ Offline-first (works without internet)
  - ✅ Fast performance
- **Files:** `src/components/GanttView/GanttTimeline.tsx` (lines 1-215), `src/components/GanttView/TimelineScale.tsx`, `src/components/GanttView/TaskBar.tsx`, `src/components/GanttView/DependencyLine.tsx`
- **Priority:** **P0** (Auto-shift dependencies, Critical path, Baseline tracking), **P1** (Progress %, Milestones), **P2** (Export, Subitems)
- **Verdict:** MVP-level Timeline with good basic visualization, but missing ALL professional project management features
- **Sources:** [Monday.com Gantt](https://support.monday.com/hc/en-us/articles/360015643840-The-Gantt-Chart-View-and-Widget), [Asana Gantt](https://help.asana.com/s/article/gantt-view?language=en_US), [MS Project Gantt](https://www.projectmanager.com/blog/microsoft-project-gantt-chart)

## FOUNDATION & INFRASTRUCTURE

### Current Foundation (✅ Completed)
- **Tech Stack:** React 19, TypeScript 5.9, Vite 7, Zustand 5, Tailwind 3, IndexedDB
- **Architecture:** Local-first, zero-cloud by default, privacy-first
- **Data Layer:** IndexedDB with Zustand state management
- **Validation:** Zod schemas for data integrity
- **Error Handling:** Error boundaries, recovery UI
- **Import/Export:** .brain format, ICS import, CSV export (planned)
- **Performance:** 60KB bundle size (vs 2-4MB competitors)
- **Offline:** Full offline functionality

### Infrastructure Gaps
⬜ **End-to-End Encryption for Sync**
- **Status:** Planned 2026
- **Implementation:** WebCrypto API, user keys
- **Priority:** HIGH (privacy differentiator)

⬜ **Service Worker for Offline Automation**
- **Use Case:** Run automations without browser open
- **Examples:** Daily task creation, reminders
- **Implementation:** Service Worker + Background Sync API
- **Priority:** HIGH (for automation features)

⬜ **IndexedDB Query Optimization**
- **Current:** Basic IndexedDB usage
- **Needed:** Compound indexes, full-text search
- **Priority:** MEDIUM

⬜ **Plugin System**
- **Inspiration:** Obsidian plugins
- **Use Case:** User-created extensions
- **Implementation:** Sandboxed JavaScript API
- **Priority:** LOW (future)

⬜ **Theme System**
- **Current:** Single theme
- **Needed:** Multiple themes, user-created
- **Priority:** LOW

⬜ **Export Formats**
- **Current:** .brain backup
- **Needed:** JSON, Markdown, CSV, PDF
- **Priority:** MEDIUM

⬜ **Import From Competitors**
- **Planned:** Trello, Asana, Todoist
- **Status:** Some work started
- **Priority:** HIGH (acquisition funnel)

## MARKET POSITIONING

### Neuman Brain's Blue Ocean Opportunity
**No competitor combines:**
1. ✅ Professional project management tools
2. ⬜ Personal habit tracking + gamification
3. ✅ Local-first privacy (no forced login)
4. ⬜ Offline automation
5. ✅ 100% free (no paywalls)
6. ✅ Fast (60KB vs 2-4MB)

### Cost Advantage
| Competitor | Annual Cost (10 users) |
|------------|------------------------|
| Monday.com | $1,440 - $2,400 |
| ClickUp | $1,080 - $2,280 |
| Asana | $1,620 - $3,660 |
| Todoist | $480 - $840 |
| Toggl Track | $1,080 - $2,160 |
| **Neuman Brain** | **$0** |

### Speed Advantage
- **NB:** 60KB bundle, <1s load time
- **Competitors:** 2-4MB, 3-10s load time
- **Result:** 10x-40x faster

### Privacy Advantage
- **NB:** Local-first by default, no tracking, optional E2EE sync
- **Competitors:** Cloud-only, data on their servers, telemetry

3. **Local AI models?** - Feasibility for on-device processing
4. **CRDT library?** - For future collaboration

---

## SUCCESS METRICS

- **Target:** Users switching from paid competitors
- **Funnel:** Import tools from Monday, ClickUp, Asana, Trello, Todoist

### Feature Parity
- **Goal:** Match 80% of Asana/Notion features (core 20% use cases)

### Performance

- **Current:** 60KB ✅

- **Goal:** <100ms interaction latency

### Privacy
- **Goal:** Zero telemetry by default
- **Current:** ✅ Achieved
- **Goal:** E2EE sync by Q2 2026

lines: - **Verdict:** MVP-level Timeline with good basic visualization, but missing ALL professional project management features
+  - ✅ Custom intervals (every 2 weeks, every 3 months, etc.)
+  - ✅ Days of week selection (for weekly recurrence)
+  - ✅ Day of month selection (for monthly recurrence, 1-31)
+  - ✅ End conditions: never/after N occurrences/until date
+  - ✅ Series end detection (respects endCount and endDate)
+  - ✅ RecurrencePicker UI component integrated into CardDetailPanel
+  - ✅ Visual recurrence editor (matches Calendar Events pattern)
+  1. ❌ **Recur from Completion Date** - Tasks always recur from original date, not completion date
+     - Todoist: ✅ "every" vs "every!" syntax - "every! 3 months" recurs 3 months from completion
+     - Asana: ✅ "Periodically" option - repeats 1-30 days after task is completed
+     - **Impact:** CRITICAL for maintenance tasks, habits, and periodic reviews where completion date matters
+     - **Example:** "Change oil filter every 3 months" should be 3 months from LAST change, not original date
+  2. ❌ **Natural Language Input** - No "every Monday" or "monthly" parsing
+     - Todoist: ✅ Full natural language parsing ("every third Thursday", "monthly", "every 2 weeks on Friday")
+     - **Impact:** Slower task creation, requires clicking through UI
+  3. ❌ **Template Integration** - Recurring tasks don't use task templates
+     - ClickUp: ✅ Recurring tasks can use task templates - each instance starts with same structure/checklists
+     - Asana: ⚠️ Subtasks copy but not full templates
+     - **Impact:** Each recurring instance is identical copy - can't have dynamic checklists or varying structure
+  4. ⚠️ **Recurrence Exceptions** - Can edit instances but no visual indication
+     - Have `recurrenceException` field in types but not fully implemented in UI
+     - No "edit just this instance" vs "edit all future instances" workflow
+     - **Impact:** Confusing UX when instances are modified
+  - ✅ On-demand generation (doesn't clutter task list with future instances)
+- **Priority:** **P0** (Recur from completion), **P1** (Natural language, Template integration), **P2** (Exception handling UI)
+- **Verdict:** Strong recurrence engine with professional patterns, but missing key flexibility (completion-based recurrence) that users expect

content.text:
# Start Task - CLAUDE.md Compliance Reminder

**This is an automated reminder to follow the CLAUDE.md workflow.**

---

## 🚨 BEFORE YOU START CODING

### Step 1: Sync with Main Branch (Rule #11)
```bash
git fetch origin main && git merge origin/main --no-edit
```

### Step 2: Read CLAUDE.md (Rules #1-11)
- Open and review `CLAUDE.md` in the root directory
- Pay special attention to rules #1-11 (IMMUTABLE)
- Review Core Development Principles (Privacy, Security, UX, Performance)

### Step 3: Create Task File (Rule #1)
```bash
# Create new task file using template
cp .claude/templates/task-template.md tasks/[feature-name].md
```

**Replace `[feature-name]` with descriptive name:**
- ✅ Good: `tasks/add-search-functionality.md`
- ✅ Good: `tasks/fix-calendar-timezone-bug.md`
- ❌ Bad: `tasks/todo.md`
- ❌ Bad: `tasks/task1.md`

### Step 4: Fill Out Task Plan (Rule #2)
Open `tasks/[feature-name].md` and complete:

**Required Sections:**
- [ ] **Overview** - What problem are you solving?
- [ ] **Problem Statement** - Detailed explanation
- [ ] **Files to Read** - List all files you need to understand
- [ ] **Proposed Changes** - Detailed technical plan
- [ ] **Todo Checklist** - Break down work into specific, actionable items with time estimates

**Use the workflow checklist for guidance:**
```bash
# Reference this while planning
.claude/workflow-checklist.md
```

### Step 5: Get User Approval (Rule #3)
⚠️ **CRITICAL: DO NOT START CODING UNTIL USER APPROVES PLAN**

Use the `ExitPlanMode` tool to present your plan and get approval.

### Step 6: Begin Implementation (Rule #4)
Only after user approval:
- Mark tasks as `in_progress` as you work
- Mark tasks as `completed` immediately when done
- Update the todo list frequently (not in batches)

### Step 7: Add Review Section When Done (Rule #7)
When ALL work is complete, add comprehensive review section to task file:
- Summary of changes
- Files created/modified (with line numbers)
- Testing status
- Issues encountered
- Time comparison (estimated vs actual)

### Step 8: Update Documentation (Rule #10)
If you made architectural changes, update:
- `docs/technical/` - New systems, patterns, architecture
- `docs/product/feature_map.md` - New features shipped
- Any other relevant docs

### Step 9: Run Tests & Checks
```bash
npm run type-check    # TypeScript check
npm run lint          # ESLint check
npm run build         # Verify bundle size < 100KB
```

---

## 📋 Quick Checklist

- [ ] Synced with main branch (`git fetch origin main && git merge origin/main --no-edit`)
- [ ] Read CLAUDE.md rules #1-11
- [ ] Created task file: `tasks/[feature-name].md`
- [ ] Filled out all required sections
- [ ] Got user approval via `ExitPlanMode` tool
- [ ] **ONLY THEN** begin coding

---

## 📚 Resources

- **Full Workflow:** See `CLAUDE.md` in root directory
- **Detailed Checklist:** See `.claude/workflow-checklist.md`
- **Task Template:** See `.claude/templates/task-template.md`
- **Automation Guide:** See `.claude/automation-guide.md`

---

## 🔧 Automation Tools

**Git Pre-Commit Hook:**
- Automatically blocks commits without task file updates
- Located at `.git/hooks/pre-commit`
- Emergency bypass: `git commit --no-verify` (use sparingly!)

**Custom Commands:**
- `/start-task` - This reminder (you're here!)
- Add more commands as needed in `.claude/commands/`

---

## ⚠️ Common Mistakes to Avoid

1. ❌ Starting to code without creating task file (Rule #1)
2. ❌ Starting to code without user approval (Rule #3)
3. ❌ Forgetting to add review section when done (Rule #7)
4. ❌ Not updating documentation for architectural changes (Rule #10)
5. ❌ Not syncing with main at start of session (Rule #11)
6. ❌ Being lazy - always find root cause, no temporary fixes (Rule #8)
7. ❌ Making complex changes - keep it simple, minimal code impact (Rule #9)

---

## 🎯 Remember the Core Rules

1. **Think → Plan → Write to tasks/[feature].md** - Create task file FIRST
2. **Plan has todo list** - Checklist with items to check off
3. **Check with me before working** - Get approval via ExitPlanMode
4. **Begin working, mark tasks complete** - Update todos as you go
5. **High-level explanations only** - Don't overexplain every detail
6. **Make changes as simple as possible** - Minimal code impact
7. **Add review section** when done - Document what changed
8. **DO NOT BE LAZY** - Find root cause, no temporary fixes
9. **Simplicity is key** - Impact minimal code, avoid bugs
10. **Update documentation** - Keep docs detailed, clear, clean
11. **Sync with main** at start of EVERY session - Stay up to date

---

**Ready to start? Follow the checklist above!** ✅

Based on the search results, here's information about task dependencies features in Asana and ClickUp with implementation details:

## **ClickUp Task Dependencies**

### Key Features:
ClickUp offers two types of dependency relationships: "Blocks" (a task preventing another task from being completed) and "Blocked by or waiting on" (a task with at least one other task that must be completed first).

### Implementation Details:

**Creating Dependencies:**
In task view 4.0, you can create dependency relationships from several places on tasks by clicking "Relate or create tasks" or the plus icon, then selecting "This task blocks" or "This task is blocked by," and then searching, browsing, or creating a new task.

**Availability:**
Dependency relationships are available on all plans, and everyone, including guests with edit or full permissions, can create task dependencies.

**Advanced Features:**
- When the Dependency Warning ClickApp is enabled, ClickUp will display an incomplete warning if you attempt to close a task that is waiting on another task
- When the Reschedule Dependencies ClickApp is activated, tasks with dependency relationships will be rescheduled automatically - for example, if Task A's due date is moved one week into the future, ClickUp automatically updates Task B's start and due dates one week into the future

**Visualization:**
In Gantt view, dependency relationships are displayed as arrows connecting tasks, with the direction of the arrow representing the type of dependency - for example, if task A is blocking task B, the arrow will start at task A and end at task B.

## **Asana Task Dependencies**

### Key Features:
In Asana, you begin by opening the task where you want to establish a dependency, then navigate to the task details and find the 'Dependencies' section within the sidebar, where you can add dependencies by selecting either 'Blocking' or 'Waiting On'.

### User Experience:
Asana offers a user-friendly interface with a straightforward way to create and visualize dependencies.

Both platforms provide comprehensive dependency management capabilities, with ClickUp offering more advanced automation features like automatic rescheduling, while Asana focuses on simplicity and ease of use.

I've found comprehensive information about PKM tools comparing Obsidian, Roam Research, and Logseq for 2025. Here's what the search reveals:

## Key PKM Tools Comparison (2025)

### **Obsidian**
Obsidian treats your knowledge base as a collection of interconnected documents, similar to a personal Wikipedia, with an interface that resembles a traditional markdown editor with powerful linking capabilities.

**Key Features:**
- Powerful linking features with graph view that maps out interconnections of thoughts
- Vast array of community-contributed plugins for customization
- Operates on Markdown concept, offers graph view, canvas to map out ideas, tabs, and split view
- Base app is free but monetizes through services

### **Logseq**
Logseq takes inspiration from Roam Research and adopts an outliner-centric model with block-based organization, daily journal workflow, bi-directional linking at the block level, and native TODO tracking.

**Key Features:**
- Celebrates innovative whiteboard functionality promoting dynamic and visual knowledge organization
- Open-source tool built on principles of transparency and flexibility
- Entirely free
- Operates on local-first principle with data stored directly on your device

### **Roam Research**
Roam Research pioneered the bidirectional linking revolution that influenced Obsidian, Logseq, and countless others, remaining a powerful PKM tool for those who embrace its outliner-based, block-reference-heavy approach.

**Key Features:**
- Daily notes page and network of interconnected thoughts make it excellent for researchers, writers, and academics
- High price ($15/month) and dated interface
- Cloud storage and collaborative features like Kanban boards and Pomodoro timers

### **Comparison Summary**
Both tools ensure you're never locked in—your notes remain readable plain text files.(no content)Both have excellent communities, but Obsidian's is larger with more resources.(no content)Beginners typically find Obsidian more approachable initially.

Based on my web search, here's what users want in productivity dashboard features for 2025:

## Core Features Users Want

### Real-Time Capabilities
Users want immediate insights into project metrics through real-time dashboards that update instantly, as these visualizations help teams make quick, informed decisions and track progress effectively.(no content)Live updates with real-time data are essential to enable timely decision-making, along with interactive visualizations that allow users to drill down into data and explore different perspectives.

### Customization & Personalization
Users want to decide what, when, where, and how they view different areas of their data dashboard, with customization being intuitive and easy to learn through drag-and-drop functionality.(no content)Users expect the ability to customize dashboards to their specific needs, with AI and machine learning delivering personalized insights.

### Collaboration Features
Real-time collaboration and flexible permission settings are crucial for seamless document sharing and teamwork, with 87% of reviewers rating this feature as important or highly important.

### Data Management
Organized document storage, version control, and easy retrieval for maintaining productivity are emphasized as important, with 86% of reviewers rating this feature as important or highly important.(no content)The ability to easily import and export data in various formats facilitates integration and efficient data handling, rated as important by 84% of reviewers.

### Visualization & Interface
The best dashboard software incorporates interactive visuals and templates to help beginners get started.(no content)Data hierarchy prioritizing critical information presented prominently, along with clear and consistent visual elements like color palettes, fonts, and icons are essential.

### Work Tracking & Metrics
Users focus on 5 must-track metrics that actually influence results: focus time, throughput, quality, engagement, and workload balance.(no content)Users want to quickly and easily spot bottlenecks, delays, problems, missed deadlines, resource warnings, and other indicators for their projects to help make decisions.

### Accessibility & Responsiveness
Dashboards should adapt seamlessly to different screen sizes and devices, with touch-friendly interactions optimized for mobile devices.(no content)Inclusive design considering users with disabilities, sufficient color contrast for readability, and keyboard navigation for users who cannot use a mouse are expected.

This represents what current research shows users prioritize in productivity dashboards for 2025, with emphasis on real-time insights, personalization, and ease of use.

(2025)","url":"https://thebusinessdive.com/notion-vs-coda"},{"title":"Coda vs. Notion: Which Workspace Tool Wins in 2025?","url":"https://clickup.com/blog/coda-vs-notion/"}]

I've completed the web search for comprehensive information about all-in-one productivity platforms in 2025, focusing on Notion, Monday, Coda, and Airtable. Here are the key findings:

## **Core Platform Capabilities**

### **Notion**
Notion is a versatile productivity tool that integrates notes, tasks, databases, and calendars into a single workspace with a highly customizable platform and intuitive interface. Notion provides an extensive library of pre-built templates that cater to a wide range of needs, from project management to personal productivity, streamlining the setup process. It offers over 10,000 templates across categories including org charts, pitch decks, expense trackers and resumes.

### **Coda**
Coda offers a unique blend of documents, spreadsheets, and databases in a single flexible platform, reimagining how teams can create, collaborate, and organize information. Coda lets you create project management tools with a formula editor, buttons, and automations without code, and these tools live inside your Coda docs using tables, buttons, and automations to build solutions like client portals or sales CRMs.

### **Airtable**
Airtable is best described as a cloud-based spreadsheet combining the familiarity of Excel or Google Sheets with the power of a relational database, enabling linking records across multiple tables. Airtable is a spreadsheet-database hybrid tool that can help you keep track of projects, tasks, and progress.

## **Key Feature Comparisons**

### **Collaboration & Teamwork**
Notion shines with real-time editing, task management, and comment functionalities that enhance teamwork, supporting seamless communication within shared documents and projects. Coda gives you everything you expect from Google Docs but with more flexibility, allowing you to add interactive elements like buttons, checklists, and real-time task updates.

### **Customization & Flexibility**
Notion offers a high degree of customization for organizing content, allowing users to create pages and sub-pages with flexible layout, enabling detailed and personalized organization according to specific needs. Coda allows users to construct documents using various elements from text and tables to buttons and automations, enabling highly tailored workspaces.

### **AI Integration**
Notion and Airtable have integrated AI into their tools, and similar to ChatGPT, each service's AI can draft blog posts, create project overviews and generate other types of content. Coda AI utilizes artificial intelligence to help edit, write, summarize, and paraphrase content, and can help brainstorm projects, analyze customers, summarize meetings, and convert text into tables.

### **Pricing & Affordability**
Notion is more affordable, and getting an equally capable project management tool that's more versatile and cheaper is always a plus. Notion's pricing follows a tiered approach and offers a free plan, with costs increasing with added features or team members.

## **Best Use Cases**

Notion handles content and marketing teams well with editorial calendar databases, pages for briefs, linked views for channels, and wikis for brand docs, making it advantageous when you need writers, designers and stakeholders to easily collaborate. Coda really shines when teams want to build internal tools without code, allowing creation of feature scoring tools, resourcing calculators, or complex operations dashboards that pull data from multiple systems.

As one Reddit user put it, Notion is better for handling documents and wikis; Coda is better for databases and automation.

content.text:
# Start Task - CLAUDE.md Compliance Reminder

**This is an automated reminder to follow the CLAUDE.md workflow.**

---

## 🚨 BEFORE YOU START CODING

### Step 1: Sync with Main Branch (Rule #11)
```bash
git fetch origin main && git merge origin/main --no-edit
```

### Step 2: Read CLAUDE.md (Rules #1-11)
- Open and review `CLAUDE.md` in the root directory
- Pay special attention to rules #1-11 (IMMUTABLE)
- Review Core Development Principles (Privacy, Security, UX, Performance)

### Step 3: Create Task File (Rule #1)
```bash
# Create new task file using template
cp .claude/templates/task-template.md tasks/[feature-name].md
```

**Replace `[feature-name]` with descriptive name:**
- ✅ Good: `tasks/add-search-functionality.md`
- ✅ Good: `tasks/fix-calendar-timezone-bug.md`
- ❌ Bad: `tasks/todo.md`
- ❌ Bad: `tasks/task1.md`

### Step 4: Fill Out Task Plan (Rule #2)
Open `tasks/[feature-name].md` and complete:

**Required Sections:**
- [ ] **Overview** - What problem are you solving?
- [ ] **Problem Statement** - Detailed explanation
- [ ] **Files to Read** - List all files you need to understand
- [ ] **Proposed Changes** - Detailed technical plan
- [ ] **Todo Checklist** - Break down work into specific, actionable items with time estimates

**Use the workflow checklist for guidance:**
```bash
# Reference this while planning
.claude/workflow-checklist.md
```

### Step 5: Get User Approval (Rule #3)
⚠️ **CRITICAL: DO NOT START CODING UNTIL USER APPROVES PLAN**

Use the `ExitPlanMode` tool to present your plan and get approval.

### Step 6: Begin Implementation (Rule #4)
Only after user approval:
- Mark tasks as `in_progress` as you work
- Mark tasks as `completed` immediately when done
- Update the todo list frequently (not in batches)

### Step 7: Add Review Section When Done (Rule #7)
When ALL work is complete, add comprehensive review section to task file:
- Summary of changes
- Files created/modified (with line numbers)
- Testing status
- Issues encountered
- Time comparison (estimated vs actual)

### Step 8: Update Documentation (Rule #10)
If you made architectural changes, update:
- `docs/technical/` - New systems, patterns, architecture
- `docs/product/feature_map.md` - New features shipped
- Any other relevant docs

### Step 9: Run Tests & Checks
```bash
npm run type-check    # TypeScript check
npm run lint          # ESLint check
npm run build         # Verify bundle size < 100KB
```

---

## 📋 Quick Checklist

- [ ] Synced with main branch (`git fetch origin main && git merge origin/main --no-edit`)
- [ ] Read CLAUDE.md rules #1-11
- [ ] Created task file: `tasks/[feature-name].md`
- [ ] Filled out all required sections
- [ ] Got user approval via `ExitPlanMode` tool
- [ ] **ONLY THEN** begin coding

---

## 📚 Resources

- **Full Workflow:** See `CLAUDE.md` in root directory
- **Detailed Checklist:** See `.claude/workflow-checklist.md`
- **Task Template:** See `.claude/templates/task-template.md`
- **Automation Guide:** See `.claude/automation-guide.md`

---

## 🔧 Automation Tools

**Git Pre-Commit Hook:**
- Automatically blocks commits without task file updates
- Located at `.git/hooks/pre-commit`
- Emergency bypass: `git commit --no-verify` (use sparingly!)

**Custom Commands:**
- `/start-task` - This reminder (you're here!)
- Add more commands as needed in `.claude/commands/`

---

## ⚠️ Common Mistakes to Avoid

1. ❌ Starting to code without creating task file (Rule #1)
2. ❌ Starting to code without user approval (Rule #3)
3. ❌ Forgetting to add review section when done (Rule #7)
4. ❌ Not updating documentation for architectural changes (Rule #10)
5. ❌ Not syncing with main at start of session (Rule #11)
6. ❌ Being lazy - always find root cause, no temporary fixes (Rule #8)
7. ❌ Making complex changes - keep it simple, minimal code impact (Rule #9)

---

## 🎯 Remember the Core Rules

1. **Think → Plan → Write to tasks/[feature].md** - Create task file FIRST
2. **Plan has todo list** - Checklist with items to check off
3. **Check with me before working** - Get approval via ExitPlanMode
4. **Begin working, mark tasks complete** - Update todos as you go
5. **High-level explanations only** - Don't overexplain every detail
6. **Make changes as simple as possible** - Minimal code impact
7. **Add review section** when done - Document what changed
8. **DO NOT BE LAZY** - Find root cause, no temporary fixes
9. **Simplicity is key** - Impact minimal code, avoid bugs
10. **Update documentation** - Keep docs detailed, clear, clean
11. **Sync with main** at start of EVERY session - Stay up to date

---

**Ready to start? Follow the checklist above!** ✅


ARGUMENTS: What do you think is best to make sure nothing is missed and everything is updated properly? Time limits / messages are not a concern. Do not be lazy per CLAUDE.md, no need to accelerate anything on my behalf.

This session is being continued from a previous conversation that ran out of context. The conversation is summarized below:
Analysis:
Let me chronologically analyze this conversation to ensure I capture everything comprehensively:

1. **Initial Request**: User asked to revise `tasks/master-implementation-status.md` to:
   - Accurately update with current codebase state
   - Include all information from public documentation for 100%+ parity
   - Not mark features as complete unless they achieve 100%+ parity with ALL competitors

2. **My Initial Approach**: I created a task file and started systematic competitive analysis, reading implementation code and researching competitors.

3. **First Iteration**: I began auditing features and discovered a pattern - features marked "COMPLETED" were actually MVP-level, not 100%+ parity.

4. **User Feedback #1**: "Never speed up or do lighter anything. Always be thorough. First update master doc with findings, then continue with full depth."

5. **User Feedback #2**: "Yes - also research and scour the internet for services, softwares, platforms, features, etc. and get it all added to master-implementation-status.md"

6. **User Feedback #3**: "What do you think is best? Time/messages not a concern. Do not be lazy per CLAUDE.md."

**Technical Work Completed**:
- Audited Kanban Board - found gaps (assignees, file attachments, WIP limits)
- Audited Timeline/Gantt - found gaps (auto-shift, critical path, baseline)
- Audited Recurring Tasks - found gaps (recur from completion, natural language)
- Updated master-implementation-status.md with detailed entries for all 3
- Discovered TaskDependency types exist but only SimpleTaskDependency is implemented

**Key Discovery**: Type definitions show professional dependency types (FS/SS/FF/SF + lag) exist but aren't implemented - just simple blocks/blocked-by.

**Current State**: Was about to continue with remaining 15+ features when user confirmed to continue thoroughly without rushing.

Summary:
1. **Primary Request and Intent**:
   - Revise `tasks/master-implementation-status.md` to accurately reflect current codebase state
   - Ensure document has ALL information from public documentation to achieve 100%+ parity with competitors
   - Reclassify any features marked "COMPLETED" that don't actually achieve 100%+ parity with ALL softwares/services/platforms in their field
   - Research and scour the internet for ALL features users might want in this productivity platform
   - Add all discovered features to appropriate locations in master-implementation-status.md
   - Be thorough and not lazy - time/messages are not a concern
   - Use full depth analysis for all remaining features

2. **Key Technical Concepts**:
   - **100%+ Parity Standard**: Features must match or exceed baseline functionality of ALL major competitors, not just one
   - **MVP vs Professional Grade**: Distinguishing between "works" and "professional-level implementation"
   - **Competitive Analysis Methodology**: Research top 3-5 competitors per feature category, create comparison matrices
   - **Task Dependencies**: Two type systems discovered - `TaskDependency` (professional: FS/SS/FF/SF + lag) and `SimpleTaskDependency` (basic: blocks/blocked-by)
   - **Recurring Tasks**: Patterns include frequency, interval, days of week, completion-based vs date-based
   - **Local-first Architecture**: Offline-first, privacy-focused, IndexedDB storage
   - **Tech Stack**: React 19, TypeScript 5.9, Vite 7, Zustand 5, Tailwind 3, @dnd-kit for drag-drop

3. **Files and Code Sections**:

   - **`tasks/master-implementation-status.md`** (MODIFIED - Multiple times)
     - **Why Important**: Central document tracking all features and their parity status for MVP
     - **Changes Made**: 
       - Moved Kanban Board from COMPLETED to PARTIAL section with comprehensive audit details
       - Moved Timeline/Gantt from COMPLETED to PARTIAL section with gap analysis
       - Added Recurring Tasks to PARTIAL section with detailed competitive comparison
       - Updated "Task Management Advanced Features" section to reference audited features
     - **Key Addition Example**:
     ```markdown
     🔶 **Kanban Board** (Status: PARTIAL - Reclassified 2025-11-28)
     - **Audit Date:** 2025-11-28
     - **Competitors Analyzed:** Trello, Asana Board View, Monday.com Kanban View
     - **Current Features:**
       - ✅ Drag-and-drop between columns (@dnd-kit with pointer + touch sensors)
       [... 20+ features listed ...]
     - **Critical Gaps (Preventing 100%+ Parity):**
       1. ❌ **Assignees/Members** - Cannot assign tasks to team members
          - Trello: ✅ Multiple assignees per card
          - Asana: ✅ Single assignee + followers
          - Monday.com: ✅ Multiple people per task
          - **Impact:** MAJOR blocker for team collaboration
     ```

   - **`tasks/revise-master-implementation-status.md`** (CREATED)
     - **Why Important**: Task planning file following CLAUDE.md workflow
     - **Content**: Overview, problem statement, files to read, proposed changes, todo checklist
     
   - **`tasks/feature-parity-audit-findings.md`** (CREATED)
     - **Why Important**: Detailed audit findings with comparison matrices
     - **Content**: Two complete feature audits (Kanban, Timeline/Gantt) with full competitor analysis
   
   - **`src/widgets/Kanban.tsx`** (READ - Lines 1-400+)
     - **Why Important**: Core Kanban implementation - needed to verify what features exist
     - **Key Code Pattern Found**:
     ```typescript
     const filteredTasks = useMemo(() => {
       return tasks.filter((task) => {
         // Search filter (title, description, or card number)
         if (searchQuery) {
           const query = searchQuery.toLowerCase();
           const cardNumberStr = task.cardNumber ? `KAN-${task.cardNumber}`.toLowerCase() : '';
           // ... filtering logic
         }
       });
     }, [tasks, searchQuery, selectedPriorities, selectedTags]);
     ```
   
   - **`src/widgets/Kanban/CardDetailPanel.tsx`** (READ - Lines 1-300+)
     - **Why Important**: Shows all task detail fields - critical for feature completeness assessment
     - **Key Features Found**: Checklists, comments, subtasks, dependencies UI, effort estimates, custom fields, time tracking integration

   - **`src/components/GanttView/GanttTimeline.tsx`** (READ - Lines 1-215)
     - **Why Important**: Timeline/Gantt implementation - shows swimlanes, dependencies, drag-resize
     - **Key Code Pattern**:
     ```typescript
     // Swimlanes by status
     const swimlanes = statuses.map((status) => ({
       status,
       tasks: groupedTasks[status] || [],
     }));
     
     // Dependency arrows (SVG overlay)
     <svg className="absolute top-0 left-0 pointer-events-none">
       {dependencies.map((dep) => (
         <DependencyLine ... />
       ))}
     </svg>
     ```

   - **`src/services/taskRecurrence.ts`** (READ - Full file, 201 lines)
     - **Why Important**: Recurring tasks engine - needed to verify recurrence capabilities
     - **Key Functions**:
       ```typescript
       export function calculateNextOccurrence(task: Task, fromDate: string): string | null {
         const { frequency, interval, daysOfWeek, dayOfMonth, endType, endDate } = task.recurrence;
         // Supports: daily, weekly (with daysOfWeek), monthly (with dayOfMonth), yearly
         // End conditions: never, after N, until date
       }
       ```

   - **`src/types/index.ts`** (READ - Multiple sections)
     - **Why Important**: Type definitions reveal both implemented and planned features
     - **Critical Discovery**:
     ```typescript
     // Professional dependency type (DEFINED but not fully implemented)
     export type DependencyType =
       | 'finish-to-start'  // Task B starts when A finishes (default)
       | 'start-to-start'   // Task B starts when A starts
       | 'finish-to-finish' // Task B finishes when A finishes
       | 'start-to-finish'; // Task B finishes when A starts (rare)
     
     export interface TaskDependency {
       taskId: string;
       type: DependencyType;
       lag: number; // Lag time in days (can be negative for lead)
     }
     
     // Simple version (CURRENTLY IMPLEMENTED)
     export interface SimpleTaskDependency {
       id: string;
       sourceTaskId: string; // Task that blocks
       targetTaskId: string; // Task that is blocked
       createdAt: string;
     }
     ```

4. **Errors and Fixes**:
   - **No errors encountered** - Work has been systematic file reading and web research
   - **User Feedback Corrections**:
     - Initially suggested faster approach → User corrected: "Never speed up, always be thorough"
     - Asked about efficiency options → User clarified: "Time/messages not a concern, do not be lazy"
     - Confirmed to continue with full depth for ALL remaining features

5. **Problem Solving**:
   - **Pattern Identified**: 100% of audited "COMPLETED" features (3/3) needed reclassification to PARTIAL
     - Kanban: Missing assignees, file attachments, WIP limits, card covers
     - Timeline/Gantt: Missing auto-shift dependencies, critical path, baseline, progress %, milestones
     - Recurring Tasks: Missing recur-from-completion, natural language input, template integration
   - **Root Cause**: Initial classifications used "works" standard instead of "100%+ parity with ALL competitors" standard
   - **Solution**: Systematic competitive research + codebase verification for each feature
   - **Ongoing**: Need to audit 15+ more features and research additional missing features across all categories

6. **All User Messages**:
   - "Let's revise tasks/master-implementation-status.md to make sure... 1) accurately updated with current state 2) has all information from public documentation for 100%+ parity 3) doesn't mark features as started as fully completed if they don't achieve 100%+ parity"
   - "Yes, proceed"
   - "Never speed up or do lighter anything. Always be thorough, do not be lazy. This is a mandated guideline. Always follow CLAUDE.md - First) Update the master doc with all necessary updates from your findings to this point (#3 but more in depth), and then continue with full depth for the rest (#1 but more in depth)."
   - "Yes - also, please research and scour the internet for services, softwares, platforms, features, etc. and anything else that could potentially be something a user might want in this kind of platform and get it all added into the appropriate location of the master-implementation-status.md document."
   - "What do you think is best to make sure nothing is missed and everything is updated properly? Time limits / messages are not a concern. Do not be lazy per CLAUDE.md, no need to accelerate anything on my behalf."

7. **Pending Tasks**:
   - Complete comprehensive audit of remaining ~15 features:
     - Task Dependencies (vs Asana/ClickUp)
     - Custom Fields (vs Monday/ClickUp)
     - Notes - Backlinks/Wikilinks (vs Obsidian/Roam/Logseq)
     - Notes - Graph View (vs Obsidian/Roam/Logseq)
     - Notes - Daily Notes (vs Obsidian/Roam/Logseq)
     - Notes - Tags System (vs Bear/Notion/Obsidian)
     - Notes - Folders (vs Notion/OneNote)
     - Forms - Form Builder (vs Google Forms/Typeform/Tally)
     - Forms - Form Renderer (vs Google Forms/Typeform)
     - Forms - Form Responses & Export (vs Google Forms/Typeform)
     - Diagrams - Diagram Editor (vs Draw.io/Excalidraw/Lucidchart)
     - Time Tracking - Time Entry & Timer (vs Toggl/Clockify/Harvest)
     - Time Tracking - Billable Hours (vs Harvest/Clockify)
     - Time Tracking - Reports (vs Toggl/Clockify/Harvest)
     - Calendar - Event Management (vs Google Cal/Apple Cal)
     - Calendar - Recurring Events (vs Google Cal/Apple Cal)
     - Calendar - ICS Import (vs Google Cal/Apple Cal)
   - Research comprehensive productivity platform features from:
     - All-in-one platforms (Notion, Coda, Airtable, Monday.com, ClickUp)
     - PKM tools (Obsidian, Roam, Logseq, Craft, Reflect)
     - Specialized tools in each category
   - Add ALL discovered features to appropriate sections of master-implementation-status.md
   - Cross-reference final results with feature_map.md
   - Final verification of all changes

8. **Current Work**:
   
   Immediately before this summary request, I was:
   
   - Conducting web research on multiple fronts simultaneously:
     - All-in-one productivity platforms (Notion, Monday, Coda, Airtable) - COMPLETED search
     - Productivity dashboard features users want - COMPLETED search  
     - PKM tools comparison (Obsidian, Roam, Logseq) - COMPLETED search
     - Task dependencies implementation (Asana, ClickUp) - COMPLETED search
   
   - Had just discovered critical type definition in `src/types/index.ts`:
     ```typescript
     // Lines 53-63: Professional TaskDependency with 4 types + lag
     export type DependencyType = 'finish-to-start' | 'start-to-start' | 'finish-to-finish' | 'start-to-finish';
     export interface TaskDependency {
       taskId: string;
       type: DependencyType;
       lag: number;
     }
     
     // Lines 98-103: SimpleTaskDependency currently implemented
     export interface SimpleTaskDependency {
       id: string;
       sourceTaskId: string; // Task that blocks
       targetTaskId: string; // Task that is blocked
       createdAt: string;
     }
     ```
     This revealed professional dependency types are DEFINED but not implemented - a potential quick win.
   
   - Was preparing to continue with Task Dependencies audit when user asked for best approach
   - Had completed 3 of 20+ feature audits (Kanban, Timeline/Gantt, Recurring Tasks)
   - All 3 audited features were reclassified from COMPLETED to PARTIAL

9. **Optional Next Step**:

Research extensively to find ALL features users might want in a productivity platform 3. Add everything to the appropriate sections of master-implementation-status.md... Let me continue with parallel research and audits."

# Master Implementation Status - Neuman Brain Dashboard
**Created:** 2025-11-26
**Purpose:** Comprehensive audit of ALL features across user notes, research docs, and competitive analysis
**Status:** Draft - Awaiting User Approval
**Source Documents:**
- `tasks/Neuman Brain User Notes 11252025.txt` (User testing feedback)
- `tasks/research-document.md` (26 feature categories researched)
- `tasks/research-document-expanded-initial-dig.md` (50+ competitor analysis)

---

1. [Implementation Status Legend](#implementation-status-legend)
2. [Completed Features (100% Parity)](#completed-features-100-parity)

4. [Not Started](#not-started)
5. [Research Needed](#research-needed)
6. [Foundation & Infrastructure](#foundation--infrastructure)
7. [Competitor Feature Matrix](#competitor-feature-matrix)

---

## Implementation Status Legend

- **COMPLETED (100%)** - Feature fully implemented with all desired functionality
- **PARTIAL (<100%)** - Feature exists but missing key capabilities or polish
- **NOT STARTED** - Feature identified but no implementation yet
- **RESEARCH NEEDED** - Requires investigation before implementation planning

---

## COMPLETED FEATURES (100% Parity)

### Dashboard & Widgets
✅ **Task Summary Widget** - Dynamically updates with Quick Add (Fixed 2025-11-25)
✅ **Upcoming Events Widget** - Height fixed, displays upcoming events (Fixed 2025-11-25)
✅ **Quick Add Task Button** - Color contrast fixed (Fixed 2025-11-25)
✅ **Widget Layout System** - Flexible dashboard widget grid

### Schedule & Calendar
✅ **ICS Calendar Import** - Date format fixed, now working (Fixed 2025-11-25)
✅ **Calendar "Today" Highlighting** - Current day clearly marked
✅ **Double-Click Day Behavior** - Opens DayDetailModal with events/tasks (Fixed 2025-11-25)
  - Shows all events for selected day

  - "Add New Event" button

  - End date validation

### Notes

  - Collapse button at right edge (no overlap)
  - Expand button when collapsed
  - Resize functionality with drag handle
  - Width persists 200-500px range
✅ **Notes CRUD** - Create, read, update, delete notes
✅ **Folder Organization** - Organize notes in folders
✅ **Daily Notes Pattern** - Obsidian/Roam-style daily journaling (Implemented 2025-11-26)
  - Keyboard shortcut (Ctrl/Cmd + D) to open today's note
  - Auto-create with customizable templates
  - Calendar view showing daily notes indicators
  - Settings UI: date format, template editor, folder selection
  - Auto-links to yesterday/tomorrow notes
  - Template variables: {date}, {yesterday}, {tomorrow}, {weekday}

### Settings
✅ **Display Name Autofill Prevention** - autoComplete="off" added (Fixed 2025-11-25)
✅ **Auto-Save Folder Display** - Shows configured folder correctly (Fixed 2025-11-25)
✅ **Backup Filename Format** - Shows "Neuman" with ".brain" outside (Fixed 2025-11-25)

### Foundation & Data Integrity (2025-11-25)

### UI Polish (2025-11-26)

✅ **MonthlyTimeReport Day Detail Modal**
  - Click any day to see entries

  - "Add Time Entry" button

### AI & Terminal

✅ **Error Messaging** - Clear explanations for unsupported commands

### Tasks & Project Management
✅ **Task CRUD** - Create, read, update, delete tasks
✅ **Task Due Dates** - Tasks can have due dates (start + due dates supported)
✅ **Task Priorities** - Priority levels (low/medium/high) with color coding
✅ **Task Status** - Status tracking with customizable columns

---

## PARTIAL IMPLEMENTATION (<100% Parity)

🔶 **Kanban Board** (Status: PARTIAL - Reclassified 2025-11-28)
- **Audit Date:** 2025-11-28
- **Competitors Analyzed:** Trello, Asana Board View, Monday.com Kanban View
