# Master Implementation Status - Neuman Brain Dashboard
**Created:** 2025-11-26
**Purpose:** Comprehensive audit of ALL features across user notes, research docs, and competitive analysis
**Status:** Draft - Awaiting User Approval
**Source Documents:**
- `tasks/Neuman Brain User Notes 11252025.txt` (User testing feedback)
- `tasks/research-document.md` (26 feature categories researched)
- `tasks/research-document-expanded-initial-dig.md` (50+ competitor analysis)

---

## Table of Contents
1. [Implementation Status Legend](#implementation-status-legend)
2. [Completed Features (100% Parity)](#completed-features-100-parity)
3. [Partial Implementation (<100% Parity)](#partial-implementation-100-parity)
4. [Not Started](#not-started)
5. [Research Needed](#research-needed)
6. [Foundation & Infrastructure](#foundation--infrastructure)
7. [Competitor Feature Matrix](#competitor-feature-matrix)

---

## Implementation Status Legend

- **COMPLETED (100%)** - Feature fully implemented with all desired functionality
- **PARTIAL (<100%)** - Feature exists but missing key capabilities or polish
- **NOT STARTED** - Feature identified but no implementation yet
- **RESEARCH NEEDED** - Requires investigation before implementation planning

---

## COMPLETED FEATURES (100% Parity)

### Dashboard & Widgets
✅ **Task Summary Widget** - Dynamically updates with Quick Add (Fixed 2025-11-25)
✅ **Upcoming Events Widget** - Height fixed, displays upcoming events (Fixed 2025-11-25)
✅ **Quick Add Task Button** - Color contrast fixed (Fixed 2025-11-25)
✅ **Widget Layout System** - Flexible dashboard widget grid

### Schedule & Calendar
✅ **ICS Calendar Import** - Date format fixed, now working (Fixed 2025-11-25)
✅ **Calendar "Today" Highlighting** - Current day clearly marked
✅ **Double-Click Day Behavior** - Opens DayDetailModal with events/tasks (Fixed 2025-11-25)
  - Shows all events for selected day
  - Shows all tasks due that day with navigation
  - "Add New Event" button
  - Goes straight to add if empty
✅ **Kanban Tasks on Calendar** - Tasks with due dates appear on calendar (Fixed 2025-11-25)
  - Blue styling to distinguish from events
  - Shows title, priority, status
  - Clickable in DayDetailModal
✅ **Monthly Calendar Unification** - Summary tab calendar matches Calendar tab style (Fixed 2025-11-26)
  - Added min-h-[80px] cells
  - Clickable days open detail modal
  - Shows time entries for selected day
✅ **Sidebar Navigation** - Expandable children for Schedule sections
✅ **Event Start Date Picker** - Events can be created for any date (Fixed 2025-11-26)
  - Editable start date field
  - Defaults to clicked calendar day
  - End date validation

### Notes
✅ **Folders Sidebar** - Button overlap fixed (Fixed 2025-11-25, Enhanced 2025-11-26)
  - Collapse button at right edge (no overlap)
  - Expand button when collapsed
  - Resize functionality with drag handle
  - Width persists 200-500px range
✅ **Notes CRUD** - Create, read, update, delete notes
✅ **Folder Organization** - Organize notes in folders
✅ **Daily Notes Pattern** - Obsidian/Roam-style daily journaling (Implemented 2025-11-26)
  - Keyboard shortcut (Ctrl/Cmd + D) to open today's note
  - Auto-create with customizable templates
  - Calendar view showing daily notes indicators
  - Settings UI: date format, template editor, folder selection
  - Auto-links to yesterday/tomorrow notes
  - Template variables: {date}, {yesterday}, {tomorrow}, {weekday}

### Settings
✅ **Display Name Autofill Prevention** - autoComplete="off" added (Fixed 2025-11-25)
✅ **Auto-Save Folder Display** - Shows configured folder correctly (Fixed 2025-11-25)
✅ **Backup Filename Format** - Shows "Neuman" with ".brain" outside (Fixed 2025-11-25)
✅ **Privacy Footer Cleanup** - Removed duplicate footer (Fixed 2025-11-25)

### Foundation & Data Integrity (2025-11-25)
✅ **Data Validation Layer** - src/services/validation.ts
  - Centralized Zod schemas
  - Date key format validation (YYYY-M-D)
  - Time format validation (HH:mm)
  - Legacy date normalization
✅ **Error Boundaries** - src/components/StoreErrorBoundary.tsx
  - Recovery UI with Try Again/Clear Data/Restore
  - Wrapped critical routes
✅ **Import Safety** - src/services/brainBackup.ts
  - Pre-import validation
  - Rollback snapshot
  - Automatic date normalization
  - Invalid data filtered

### UI Polish (2025-11-26)
✅ **Modal Overflow Fixes**
  - CardDetailPanel: Removed nested scroll
  - QuickAddModal: Added max-h-[85vh]
  - Proper content containment
✅ **MonthlyTimeReport Day Detail Modal**
  - Click any day to see entries
  - Shows project colors, descriptions, durations
  - "Add Time Entry" button

### AI & Terminal
✅ **AI Terminal Shell** - Basic shell emulation with error messaging
✅ **Error Messaging** - Clear explanations for unsupported commands

### Tasks & Project Management
✅ **Task CRUD** - Create, read, update, delete tasks
✅ **Task Due Dates** - Tasks can have due dates (start + due dates supported)
✅ **Task Priorities** - Priority levels (low/medium/high) with color coding
✅ **Task Status** - Status tracking with customizable columns

---

## PARTIAL IMPLEMENTATION (<100% Parity)

### Tasks & Project Management
🔶 **Kanban Board** (Status: PARTIAL - Reclassified 2025-11-28)
- **Audit Date:** 2025-11-28
- **Competitors Analyzed:** Trello, Asana Board View, Monday.com Kanban View
- **Current Features:**
  - ✅ Drag-and-drop between columns (@dnd-kit with pointer + touch sensors)
  - ✅ Customizable columns (ColumnManager, 3-7 column view options)
  - ✅ Card numbers (KAN-#) with search support - **UNIQUE ADVANTAGE**
  - ✅ Priority levels (low/medium/high) with color coding
  - ✅ Tags with autocomplete and filtering
  - ✅ Start dates + due dates
  - ✅ Checklists with real-time persistence
  - ✅ Comments with timestamps and activity log
  - ✅ Subtasks
  - ✅ Board and List views with toggle
  - ✅ Search (by title, description, or card number)
  - ✅ Advanced filters (priority, tags) with collapsible panel
  - ✅ Archived tasks view
  - ✅ Quick Add modal (Cmd/Ctrl+K global shortcut)
  - ✅ Keyboard navigation (arrow keys, Enter, Delete, ? for help)
  - ✅ Undo/Redo with toast notifications - **UNIQUE ADVANTAGE**
  - ✅ Auto-save on blur
  - ✅ View mode persistence (localStorage)
  - ✅ Effort estimates (XS/S/M/L/XL)
  - ✅ Custom status support
  - ✅ Time estimates and tracking integration
  - ✅ Custom fields integration
  - ✅ Recurring tasks integration
  - ✅ Task dependencies (blocks/blocked-by relationships)
- **Critical Gaps (Preventing 100%+ Parity):**
  1. ❌ **Assignees/Members** - Cannot assign tasks to team members
     - Trello: ✅ Multiple assignees per card
     - Asana: ✅ Single assignee + followers
     - Monday.com: ✅ Multiple people per task
     - **Impact:** MAJOR blocker for team collaboration - baseline feature in ALL competitors
  2. ❌ **File Attachments** - Cannot attach files to cards
     - Trello: ✅ Drag-drop images/docs, cloud storage integration
     - Asana: ✅ File attachments with preview
     - Monday.com: ✅ File columns with covers (crop/fit modes)
     - **Impact:** Limits use case to simple text-only tasks - baseline in ALL competitors
  3. ❌ **WIP Limits** - Cannot set column task limits
     - Monday.com: ✅ Task limits per column with warnings
     - Trello: Available via Power-Up
     - **Impact:** Missing key Kanban methodology feature
  4. ❌ **Card Covers/Images** - No visual covers on cards
     - Trello: ✅ Image covers from attachments
     - Monday.com: ✅ File column covers (crop/fit modes)
     - **Impact:** Less visual, harder to scan board at a glance
  5. ⚠️ **Calendar View Integration** - Calendar exists but on separate page
     - Trello: Premium feature, integrated view
     - Asana: ✅ One-click view switch
     - Monday.com: ✅ Instant view toggle
     - **Impact:** Less convenient workflow, requires navigation
- **Our Unique Advantages:**
  - ✅ Card numbers (KAN-#) - Unique to us, professional tracking
  - ✅ Undo/Redo - Unique to us, prevents mistakes
  - ✅ Better keyboard shortcuts than competitors
  - ✅ Offline-first, local storage (privacy advantage)
  - ✅ No paywalls (Timeline/Gantt included for free)
  - ✅ Faster performance (60KB vs 2-4MB competitors)
- **Files:** `src/widgets/Kanban.tsx`, `src/widgets/Kanban/CardDetailPanel.tsx`, `src/widgets/Kanban/KanbanCard.tsx`, `src/widgets/Kanban/KanbanColumn.tsx`, `src/widgets/Kanban/ListView.tsx`
- **Priority:** **P0** (Assignees, File Attachments), **P1** (WIP Limits, Card Covers)
- **Verdict:** MVP-level Kanban with excellent offline/keyboard UX, but missing baseline team collaboration features
- **Sources:** [Trello Kanban Features](https://www.projectmanager.com/blog/trello-kanban-board), [Asana Board View](https://help.asana.com/s/article/board-view?language=en_US), [Monday.com Kanban](https://support.monday.com/hc/en-us/articles/360000661379-The-Kanban-View)

🔶 **Timeline/Gantt View** (Status: PARTIAL - Reclassified 2025-11-28)
- **Audit Date:** 2025-11-28
- **Competitors Analyzed:** Monday.com Gantt Chart, Asana Gantt View, Microsoft Project
- **Current Features:**
  - ✅ Task bars with start/due dates visualization
  - ✅ Drag bars to reschedule (updates startDate/dueDate in real-time)
  - ✅ Resize bars to change duration
  - ✅ 3 zoom levels (day/week/month) with responsive scale
  - ✅ Today marker with indicator dot and label
  - ✅ Swimlanes by task status (backlog/todo/inprogress/review/done) - **UNIQUE ADVANTAGE**
  - ✅ Priority-based color coding
  - ✅ Dependency arrows (SimpleTaskDependency with SVG overlay)
  - ✅ Row-based positioning for clarity
  - ✅ Responsive scrolling and empty state handling
  - ✅ Tab interface in Tasks page (Board/List/Timeline toggle)
- **Critical Gaps (Preventing 100%+ Parity):**
  1. ❌ **Auto-shift Dependent Tasks** - When parent task moves, children don't auto-adjust
     - Monday.com: ✅ "Shift all dependent tasks by however many days needed"
     - Asana: ✅ "Auto-scheduling" - blocked tasks automatically adjust dates
     - MS Project: ✅ Standard behavior, cascading date updates
     - **Impact:** Manual rescheduling required defeats purpose of dependencies - baseline in ALL competitors
  2. ❌ **Critical Path Analysis** - Cannot identify project bottlenecks
     - Monday.com: ✅ Critical path visualization with highlighting
     - Asana: ✅ Premium+ feature, shows longest sequence
     - MS Project: ✅ Core feature, red highlighting
     - **Impact:** Can't optimize project timeline or identify delays - professional PM baseline
  3. ❌ **Baseline Comparison** - Cannot track planned vs actual schedule
     - Monday.com: ✅ Baseline snapshot, green/red variance indicators
     - MS Project: ✅ Up to 10 baselines with variance tracking
     - **Impact:** No way to measure schedule variance or slippage - project management necessity
  4. ❌ **Progress Tracking (% Complete)** - No visual completion indicators on bars
     - Monday.com: ✅ Shows progress percentage within bars
     - Asana: ✅ Task percentage of completion display
     - MS Project: ✅ Progress bars within Gantt bars (filled portion)
     - **Impact:** Can't see task progress at a glance - baseline feature
  5. ❌ **Milestone Markers** - No way to mark key deadlines on timeline
     - Monday.com: ✅ Milestone tracking with diamond markers
     - Asana: ✅ Milestones as specialized tasks
     - MS Project: ✅ Diamond milestone markers on timeline
     - **Impact:** Can't highlight critical dates/deliverables - essential for project planning
  6. ❌ **Professional Dependency Types** - Only simple blocks/blocked-by
     - MS Project: ✅ 4 dependency types (FS/SS/FF/SF) + lag time
     - **Note:** Type definitions EXIST in `src/types/index.ts` (TaskDependency interface with DependencyType enum) but only SimpleTaskDependency is implemented
     - **Impact:** Can't model complex project relationships (parallel tasks, delayed starts)
  7. ❌ **Subitem Support on Timeline** - Subtasks don't appear on Gantt
     - Monday.com: ✅ Subitems visible on timeline
     - MS Project: ✅ Summary tasks with subtask rollup
     - **Impact:** Can't see task breakdown hierarchy visually
  8. ❌ **Export Timeline** - Cannot export Gantt to PDF/Excel/image
     - Monday.com: ✅ Export to Excel
     - MS Project: ✅ Export to PDF, Excel, images
     - **Impact:** Can't share timeline with stakeholders outside app
- **Our Unique Advantages:**
  - ✅ Swimlanes by status (clearer organization than Asana)
  - ✅ Integrated with Kanban in single page (seamless workflow)
  - ✅ Free (Asana Gantt is Premium+ only)
  - ✅ Offline-first (works without internet)
  - ✅ Lightweight (no heavy dependencies)
- **Files:** `src/components/GanttView/GanttTimeline.tsx`, `src/components/GanttView/TimelineScale.tsx`, `src/components/GanttView/TaskBar.tsx`, `src/components/GanttView/DependencyLine.tsx`
- **Priority:** **P0** (Auto-shift, Critical Path, Baseline), **P1** (Progress %, Milestones, Professional Dependencies), **P2** (Export, Subitem support)
- **Verdict:** MVP-level Gantt with unique swimlane visualization, but missing professional project management capabilities
- **Sources:** [Monday.com Gantt Chart](https://support.monday.com/hc/en-us/articles/360015643840-The-Gantt-Chart-View-and-Widget), [Asana Gantt](https://help.asana.com/s/article/gantt-view?language=en_US), [MS Project Gantt](https://www.projectmanager.com/blog/microsoft-project-gantt-chart)

🔶 **Recurring Tasks** (Status: PARTIAL - Audited 2025-11-28)
- **Audit Date:** 2025-11-28
- **Competitors Analyzed:** Todoist, Asana, ClickUp
- **Current Features:**
  - ✅ Frequency patterns (daily, weekly, monthly, yearly) with intervals
  - ✅ Weekly: Days of week selection (M/T/W/Th/F/Sa/Su checkboxes)
  - ✅ Monthly: Day of month specification
  - ✅ End conditions: Never / After N occurrences / Until specific date
  - ✅ Auto-generation of next instance when current is completed
  - ✅ Next occurrence calculation and display
  - ✅ Parent-instance relationship tracking (recurrenceId, isRecurringParent)
  - ✅ RecurrencePicker UI component with all options
  - ✅ Integration with Kanban CardDetailPanel
  - ✅ Instance management (view all instances of recurring task)
- **Critical Gaps (Preventing 100%+ Parity):**
  1. ❌ **Recur from Completion Date** - Only recurs from original due date
     - Todoist: ✅ "every day" (from due) vs "every! day" (from completion)
     - ClickUp: ✅ "Recur from due date" vs "Recur from completion date" toggle
     - Asana: ✅ Same behavior with toggle
     - **Impact:** Flexible tasks (e.g., "call client every 5 days") can't be modeled - major use case gap
  2. ❌ **Natural Language Input** - No "every 2 weeks on Monday" parser
     - Todoist: ✅ Smart parser ("every weekday", "every 2 weeks", "every month on the 15th")
     - ClickUp: ✅ Natural language + visual picker hybrid
     - **Impact:** Slower to set up, requires clicking through UI - UX friction
  3. ❌ **Template Integration** - Recurring tasks don't pre-fill checklists/description
     - ClickUp: ✅ Recurring tasks can use templates (checklists, custom fields, etc.)
     - Asana: ✅ Task templates can be recurring
     - **Impact:** Repetitive setup work for complex recurring tasks (e.g., weekly report with same checklist)
  4. ⚠️ **Advanced Patterns** - No "last Friday of month" or "2nd Tuesday"
     - Todoist: ✅ "every last friday" pattern
     - **Impact:** Some business patterns can't be expressed (e.g., monthly all-hands meeting on first Monday)
- **Our Advantages:**
  - ✅ Full UI integration (not hidden in advanced settings)
  - ✅ Instance relationship tracking (can see all occurrences)
  - ✅ Free (Todoist advanced recurrence requires Premium)
  - ✅ Offline-first

🔶 **Task Dependencies** (Status: PARTIAL - Audited 2025-11-28)
- **Audit Date:** 2025-11-28
- **Competitors Analyzed:** Asana, ClickUp, Monday.com
- **Current Implementation:**
  - ✅ SimpleTaskDependency: Basic blocks/blocked-by relationships
  - ✅ UI in CardDetailPanel (lines 615-750): Add blocker/blocked tasks with dropdowns
  - ✅ Visual indicators: ⚠️ Blocked By (yellow), 🔒 Blocks (blue accent)
  - ✅ Click dependency to navigate to related task
  - ✅ Remove dependencies with × button on hover
  - ✅ Circular dependency prevention in addDependency()
  - ✅ Self-dependency prevention
  - ✅ Validation: Can't complete task with incomplete blockers (useKanbanStore.ts:274-287)
  - ✅ Activity log tracking for dependency changes
  - ✅ Gantt timeline visualization (DependencyLine.tsx with SVG arrows)
- **Critical Gaps (Preventing 100%+ Parity):**
  1. ❌ **Professional Dependency Types** - Only Finish-to-Start implemented
     - **IMPORTANT:** Full TaskDependency interface DEFINED in `src/types/index.ts` but NOT USED:
       ```typescript
       export type DependencyType = 'finish-to-start' | 'start-to-start' | 'finish-to-finish' | 'start-to-finish';
       export interface TaskDependency {
         taskId: string;
         type: DependencyType;
         lag: number; // Lag time in days (can be negative for lead)
       }
       ```
     - Asana: ✅ Basic dependencies only (similar to our current)
     - ClickUp: ✅ 4 types + waiting on/blocking
     - MS Project: ✅ All 4 types (FS/SS/FF/SF) + lag/lead time
     - **Impact:** Can't model parallel work (SS), coordinated finishes (FF), or complex schedules - professional PM baseline
  2. ❌ **Auto-shift Dependent Tasks** - Date changes don't cascade (already noted in Gantt audit)
     - ClickUp: ✅ "Rescheduling dependencies" option
     - MS Project: ✅ Automatic cascading by default
     - **Impact:** Manual rescheduling burden, defeats dependency purpose
  3. ❌ **Lag/Lead Time** - No "+3 days after" or "-2 days before"
     - MS Project: ✅ Lag time in dependency definition
     - **Impact:** Can't express "start QA 2 days before dev finishes" - real-world scheduling need
  4. ❌ **Dependency Warnings** - No alerts when blocker is overdue
     - ClickUp: ✅ Warning badges when blocked by overdue tasks
     - Monday.com: ✅ Dependency status indicators
     - **Impact:** Silent failures, tasks stuck without visibility
- **Quick Win Opportunity:** TaskDependency types already defined, just need to:
  1. Add type selector to CardDetailPanel dependency UI
  2. Update addDependency() to accept type + lag parameters
  3. Implement cascading date logic in GanttTimeline
  4. Add lag time input to dependency creation
- **Files:** `src/stores/useKanbanStore.ts` (lines 1019-1105), `src/widgets/Kanban/CardDetailPanel.tsx` (lines 615-750), `src/types/index.ts` (lines 53-63, 98-103), `src/components/GanttView/DependencyLine.tsx`
- **Priority:** **P0** (Professional types + lag - types already defined!), **P1** (Auto-shift, Warnings)
- **Verdict:** Basic dependency tracking works, but professional PM features are defined but unimplemented - low-hanging fruit
- **Sources:** [Asana Dependencies](https://help.asana.com/s/article/dependencies?language=en_US), [ClickUp Task Dependencies](https://help.clickup.com/hc/en-us/articles/6305955166359-Task-Dependencies), [MS Project Dependencies](https://support.microsoft.com/en-us/office/link-tasks-in-a-project-d7c17100-6dc7-49b6-b5f1-30ed86dbd079)

### Notes & Knowledge Management
🔶 **Notes - Backlinks/Wikilinks** (Status: PARTIAL - Audited 2025-11-28)
- **Audit Date:** 2025-11-28
- **Competitors Analyzed:** Obsidian, Roam Research, Logseq
- **Current Implementation:**
  - ✅ Wiki-style link syntax: `[[Note Title]]` (LINK_PATTERN regex in backlinks.ts)
  - ✅ Link extraction from note content (`extractWikiLinks()`)
  - ✅ Link resolution: Exact + partial title matching (`resolveLinksToIds()`)
  - ✅ Backlinks panel component (BacklinksPanel.tsx) with count badge
  - ✅ Click backlink to navigate to source note
  - ✅ Empty state: "Use [[Note Title]] to create links" instruction
  - ✅ Auto-update linked notes when content changes (`updateLinkedNotes()`)
  - ✅ linkedNotes array stored on Note type
  - ✅ Backlinks panel integrated in NotesEditor
- **Critical Gaps (Preventing 100%+ Parity):**
  1. ❌ **Unlinked Mentions** - No detection of note titles mentioned without brackets
     - Obsidian: ✅ "Unlinked mentions" section showing implicit references
     - Roam: ✅ Detects unlinked references automatically
     - Logseq: ✅ Shows both linked and unlinked references
     - **Impact:** Miss implicit connections, manual linking burden
  2. ❌ **Link Autocomplete** - No `[[` trigger for note picker
     - Obsidian: ✅ Type `[[` to open note search popup
     - Roam: ✅ Instant search on `[[` with fuzzy matching
     - Logseq: ✅ Page autocomplete on `[[`
     - **Impact:** Must remember exact titles, typos create broken links
  3. ❌ **Broken Link Detection** - No warnings for links to non-existent notes
     - Obsidian: ✅ Purple color for broken links, "Create note" option
     - Roam: ✅ Auto-creates page on click
     - Logseq: ✅ Broken link styling + creation option
     - **Impact:** Silent failures, unclear if link is valid
  4. ❌ **Block-level Links** - Can't link to specific paragraphs/headings
     - Obsidian: ✅ `[[Note#Heading]]` and `[[Note^block-id]]` syntax
     - Roam: ✅ Block references with `((block-id))`
     - Logseq: ✅ Block embedding and references
     - **Impact:** Must link to entire note, not specific sections
  5. ❌ **Link Preview on Hover** - No popover showing linked note content
     - Obsidian: ✅ Hover preview with note excerpt
     - Roam: ✅ Inline preview on hover
     - **Impact:** Must click links to see content, slows research flow
- **Our Advantages:**
  - ✅ Partial matching (finds "JavaScript Basics" when linking "JavaScript")
  - ✅ Clean UI with count badges
  - ✅ Offline-first
- **Files:** `src/utils/backlinks.ts`, `src/components/BacklinksPanel.tsx`, `src/widgets/NotesEditor.tsx` (line 79), `src/stores/useNotesStore.ts`
- **Priority:** **P0** (Link autocomplete - UX blocker), **P1** (Unlinked mentions, Broken link detection), **P2** (Block links, Hover preview)
- **Verdict:** Basic bidirectional linking works, but missing PKM essentials for serious knowledge work
- **Sources:** [Obsidian Linking](https://help.obsidian.md/Linking+notes+and+files/Internal+links), [Roam Research Bidirectional Links](https://roamresearch.com/#/app/help/page/Roam_Research_White_Paper), [Logseq Backlinks](https://docs.logseq.com/#/page/contents)

🔶 **Notes - Graph View** (Status: PARTIAL - Audited 2025-11-28)
- **Audit Date:** 2025-11-28
- **Competitors Analyzed:** Obsidian, Roam Research, Logseq
- **Current Implementation:**
  - ✅ D3 force-directed graph (GraphCanvas.tsx using d3.forceSimulation)
  - ✅ Node types: Notes (circles) and Tags (differentiated)
  - ✅ Edge types: Backlinks (cyan arrows), Tag relationships
  - ✅ Zoom/pan with d3.zoom (scale 0.1-4x)
  - ✅ Drag nodes to reposition (with physics simulation)
  - ✅ Click node to navigate (onNodeClick callback)
  - ✅ Physics forces: collision, charge (repulsion), centering, link distance
  - ✅ Arrow markers for directed edges
  - ✅ Color coding: backlinks (cyan), regular edges (border color)
  - ✅ SVG-based rendering for performance
  - ✅ Graph data processing utilities (graphDataProcessor.ts)
  - ✅ Dedicated /graph-view page
- **Critical Gaps (Preventing 100%+ Parity):**
  1. ❌ **Local Graph** - No focused view for single note's connections
     - Obsidian: ✅ Local graph showing 1-2 hop neighbors
     - Roam: ✅ Page graph for current page
     - Logseq: ✅ Local graph with depth slider
     - **Impact:** Can't focus on specific note's neighborhood, overwhelming for large graphs
  2. ❌ **Filters** - No ability to filter by tags, folders, date ranges
     - Obsidian: ✅ Filter by tags, folders, paths, search terms
     - Logseq: ✅ Filter by page properties, tags, namespaces
     - **Impact:** Graph becomes unusable with 100+ notes, no way to focus
  3. ❌ **Node Sizing** - All nodes same size, no indication of importance
     - Obsidian: ✅ Node size = number of connections (degree centrality)
     - Roam: ✅ Node size based on reference count
     - **Impact:** Can't identify hub notes or important concepts visually
  4. ❌ **Link Strength** - No indication of connection frequency
     - Obsidian: ✅ Link thickness = number of references
     - **Impact:** All connections appear equal weight
  5. ❌ **Color Groups** - No automatic clustering or coloring by category
     - Obsidian: ✅ Color groups by tags, folders, or custom groups
     - Logseq: ✅ Namespace-based coloring
     - **Impact:** Hard to see thematic clusters in knowledge base
  6. ❌ **Orphan Detection** - No highlighting of isolated notes
     - Obsidian: ✅ Shows orphan notes (no connections)
     - **Impact:** Can't identify disconnected knowledge
  7. ❌ **Search in Graph** - No search to highlight specific nodes
     - Obsidian: ✅ Search bar highlights matching nodes
     - **Impact:** Must visually scan to find notes
- **Our Advantages:**
  - ✅ Tag relationships visualized (not just note-to-note)
  - ✅ Smooth physics simulation
  - ✅ Free (comparable to Obsidian which is also free for local graphs)
- **Files:** `src/components/Graph/GraphCanvas.tsx`, `src/utils/graphSimulation.ts`, `src/utils/graphDataProcessor.ts`, `src/pages/GraphView.tsx`
- **Priority:** **P0** (Local graph, Filters - unusable without these at scale), **P1** (Node sizing, Color groups), **P2** (Link strength, Search, Orphans)
- **Verdict:** Beautiful graph visualization, but impractical for actual knowledge management at scale
- **Sources:** [Obsidian Graph View](https://help.obsidian.md/Plugins/Graph+view), [Logseq Graph](https://docs.logseq.com/#/page/graph%20view), [Roam Graph](https://roamresearch.com/#/app/help/page/graph-view)

🔶 **Recurring Tasks** (Status: PARTIAL - Reclassified 2025-11-28)
- **Audit Date:** 2025-11-28
- **Competitors Analyzed:** Todoist, ClickUp, Asana
- **Current Features:**
  - ✅ Frequency patterns: daily/weekly/monthly/yearly
  - ✅ Custom intervals (every 2 weeks, every 3 months, etc.)
  - ✅ Days of week selection (for weekly recurrence)
  - ✅ Day of month selection (for monthly recurrence, 1-31)
  - ✅ End conditions: never/after N occurrences/until date
  - ✅ On-demand instance generation (not pre-generated)
  - ✅ Parent/instance relationship tracking (recurrenceId, isRecurringParent)
  - ✅ Next occurrence calculation
  - ✅ Instance deduplication (prevents duplicate instances)
  - ✅ Series end detection (respects endCount and endDate)
  - ✅ RecurrencePicker UI component integrated into CardDetailPanel
  - ✅ Visual recurrence editor (matches Calendar Events pattern)
  - ✅ Full RRULE-like pattern support
- **Critical Gaps (Preventing 100%+ Parity):**
  1. ❌ **Recur from Completion Date** - Tasks always recur from original date, not completion date
     - Todoist: ✅ "every" vs "every!" syntax - "every! 3 months" recurs 3 months from completion
     - Asana: ✅ "Periodically" option - repeats 1-30 days after task is completed
     - ClickUp: ✅ "Recur only after completion" checkbox
     - **Impact:** CRITICAL for maintenance tasks, habits, and periodic reviews where completion date matters
     - **Example:** "Change oil filter every 3 months" should be 3 months from LAST change, not original date
  2. ❌ **Natural Language Input** - No "every Monday" or "monthly" parsing
     - Todoist: ✅ Full natural language parsing ("every third Thursday", "monthly", "every 2 weeks on Friday")
     - ClickUp: Partial (date picker focused)
     - Asana: ❌ No natural language
     - **Impact:** Slower task creation, requires clicking through UI
  3. ❌ **Template Integration** - Recurring tasks don't use task templates
     - ClickUp: ✅ Recurring tasks can use task templates - each instance starts with same structure/checklists
     - Asana: ⚠️ Subtasks copy but not full templates
     - Todoist: ❌ No templates
     - **Impact:** Each recurring instance is identical copy - can't have dynamic checklists or varying structure
  4. ⚠️ **Recurrence Exceptions** - Can edit instances but no visual indication
     - Have `recurrenceException` field in types but not fully implemented in UI
     - No "edit just this instance" vs "edit all future instances" workflow
     - **Impact:** Confusing UX when instances are modified
- **Our Unique Advantages:**
  - ✅ On-demand generation (doesn't clutter task list with future instances)
  - ✅ Matches Calendar Events pattern (consistent UX)
  - ✅ Offline-first (works without internet)
  - ✅ Clean parent/instance relationship model
- **Files:** `src/services/taskRecurrence.ts` (lines 1-201), `src/utils/recurrence.ts`, `src/components/RecurrencePicker.tsx`, `src/types/index.ts` (lines 209-221)
- **Priority:** **P0** (Recur from completion), **P1** (Natural language, Template integration), **P2** (Exception handling UI)
- **Verdict:** Strong recurrence engine with professional patterns, but missing key flexibility (completion-based recurrence) that users expect
- **Sources:** [Todoist Recurring Tasks](https://www.todoist.com/help/articles/introduction-to-recurring-dates-YUYVJJAV), [ClickUp Recurring Tasks](https://help.clickup.com/hc/en-us/articles/6309885016471-Use-recurring-tasks), [Asana Recurring Tasks](https://blog.coupler.io/asana-recurring-tasks/)

### Forms & Data Collection
🔶 **Form Builder** (Status: PARTIAL - Reclassified 2025-11-28)
- **Audit Date:** 2025-11-28
- **Competitors Analyzed:** Google Forms, Typeform, Tally
- **Current Features:**
  - ✅ 11 field types: text, textarea, number, date, time, select, multiselect, radio, checkbox, rating, scale
  - ✅ Field validation (required, min/max for numbers)
  - ✅ Drag-to-reorder fields (up/down buttons)
  - ✅ Field duplication and deletion
  - ✅ Form settings (title, description, allowMultipleSubmissions, resetPeriod)
  - ✅ Dynamic form rendering (FormRenderer component)
  - ✅ Client-side validation with error messages
  - ✅ Response storage with timestamps
  - ✅ Response viewing and CSV export
  - ✅ Forms Dashboard Widget (stats, top forms, navigation)
  - ✅ Form duplication
  - ✅ Success state after submission
- **Critical Gaps (Preventing 100%+ Parity):**
  1. ❌ **Conditional Logic/Branching** - Can't show/hide fields based on answers
     - Google Forms: ✅ Section-based branching
     - Typeform: ✅ Advanced logic with scoring, calculations
     - Tally: ✅ FREE intuitive block-based logic builder
     - **Impact:** Can't create dynamic forms that adapt to responses - major limitation for complex workflows
  2. ❌ **File Upload** - Can't collect documents, images, resumes
     - ALL 3 competitors: ✅ File upload capability
     - **Impact:** Unusable for job applications, document collection, portfolio submissions - baseline feature
  3. ❌ **Progress Bar** - Users can't see how far through form they are
     - ALL 3 competitors: ✅ Show progress indicator
     - **Impact:** Higher abandon rates on long forms - standard UX practice
  4. ❌ **Response Analytics** - No insights into form performance
     - Google Forms: ✅ Basic analytics
     - Typeform: ✅ Advanced analytics (paid)
     - Tally: ✅ Response analytics
     - **Impact:** Can't optimize forms or understand user behavior
  5. ❌ **Spam Protection** - No reCAPTCHA or honeypot
     - Typeform: ✅ Spam protection (paid)
     - Tally: ✅ Built-in spam protection
     - **Impact:** Vulnerable to spam submissions
  6. ❌ **Answer Piping** - Can't reference previous answers in questions
     - Typeform: ✅ Answer piping (paid)
     - Tally: ✅ FREE answer piping
     - **Impact:** Can't create personalized form experiences
  7. ❌ **Hidden Fields** - No UTM parameter tracking
     - Typeform: ✅ Hidden fields (paid)
     - Tally: ✅ FREE hidden fields for URL params
     - **Impact:** Can't track marketing campaigns via forms
  8. ❌ **Quiz Mode** - No auto-scoring or feedback per answer
     - Google Forms: ✅ Quiz mode with auto-scoring
     - Typeform: ✅ Quiz mode (paid)
     - Tally: ✅ Quiz features
     - **Impact:** Can't create assessments or knowledge tests
  9. ❌ **Custom Thank You Page** - No redirect after submission
     - Google Forms: ✅ Custom confirmation message
     - Typeform: ✅ Custom thank you page
     - Tally: ✅ Redirect after submission
     - **Impact:** Can't guide users to next action
- **Our Advantages:**
  - ✅ FREE (Typeform charges for logic, scoring, hidden fields)
  - ✅ Offline-first, local storage
  - ✅ Clean, minimal UI
  - ✅ Well-typed TypeScript implementation
  - ✅ No data on external servers (privacy)
- **Files:** `src/pages/FormBuilder.tsx`, `src/components/FormRenderer/FormRenderer.tsx`, `src/types/forms.ts`, `src/stores/useFormsStore.ts`
- **Priority:** **P0** (Conditional logic, File upload, Progress bar, Analytics, Spam protection), **P1** (Answer piping, Hidden fields, Quiz mode)
- **Verdict:** MVP-level form builder with core functionality, but missing advanced features users expect from modern form tools - Tally offers more features for FREE
- **Sources:** [Typeform vs Tally](https://www.typeform.com/blog/typeform-vs-tally), [Google Forms Features](https://clickydrip.com/google-forms-features/), [Form Builder Must-Haves](https://www.thehumancapitalhub.com/articles/the-7-best-form-builder-tools-for-20252026)

  - ✅ Grid background with snap-to-grid (20px, configurable)
  - ✅ Shape styling (fill, stroke, strokeWidth, strokeStyle)
  - ✅ Text formatting (fontSize, fontWeight, textAlign)
  - ✅ Smart connectors (attach to shapes)
  - ✅ Export options (PNG, SVG, PDF, JSON)
  - ✅ Auto-save (2s debounce)
  - ✅ Keyboard shortcuts (Delete, Copy/Paste, Undo/Redo, Select All, Escape)
  - ✅ Freehand drawing tool
  - ✅ Arrow markers (start/end arrows)
  - ✅ Full-screen editor at `/diagrams/:id`
  - ✅ Library view with grid/list modes, search, create/delete
- **Critical Gaps (Preventing 100%+ Parity):**
  1. ❌ **Shape Libraries** - No pre-built shapes (flowchart, UML, network, AWS, etc.)
     - Draw.io: ✅ 1000+ shapes across 50+ categories (flowcharts, UML, network diagrams, cloud architecture)
     - Lucidchart: ✅ Extensive professional shape libraries
     - Excalidraw: ✅ Built-in shape library
     - **Impact:** Users must draw everything from basic shapes - tedious for professional diagrams
  2. ❌ **Templates** - No starting points for common diagrams
     - Draw.io: ✅ 100+ free templates
     - Lucidchart: ✅ 1000+ templates (flowcharts, org charts, wireframes, network diagrams)
     - **Impact:** Slow to create standard diagram types, no best practices guidance
  3. ❌ **Grouping** - Can't group shapes to move as a unit
     - ALL 3 competitors: ✅ Support shape grouping/ungrouping
     - **Impact:** Managing complex diagrams with many shapes is painful - baseline feature
  4. ❌ **Alignment Tools** - No align left/center/right/top/bottom or distribute evenly
     - Draw.io & Lucidchart: ✅ Full alignment/distribution toolbar
     - Excalidraw: ✅ Alignment tools
     - **Impact:** Manual pixel-perfect alignment required, unprofessional-looking diagrams
  5. ❌ **Layers** - Only basic z-index, no layer management
     - Draw.io: ✅ Layer panel with show/hide/lock
     - Lucidchart: ✅ Layer management
     - **Impact:** Hard to organize complex diagrams with many overlapping elements
  6. ❌ **Auto-Layout** - No automatic arrangement for flowcharts/org charts
     - Draw.io: ✅ Auto-layout algorithms
     - Lucidchart: ✅ Smart container auto-resize
     - **Impact:** Manual layout adjustments when structure changes
  7. ❌ **Hand-drawn Style** - No Excalidraw-style sketchy rendering
     - Excalidraw: ✅ Signature hand-drawn aesthetic
     - **Impact:** All diagrams look formal/rigid, no casual brainstorming style
- **Our Advantages:**
  - ✅ Freehand drawing (Draw.io doesn't have this)
  - ✅ Clean, fast Konva rendering
  - ✅ Undo/Redo with 50 steps
  - ✅ Offline-first, local storage
  - ✅ FREE (Lucidchart is paid, $7.95/mo+)
  - ✅ Privacy-focused (no cloud, no tracking)
  - ✅ Custom-built per CLAUDE.md "Build vs Buy" philosophy
- **Files:** `src/pages/DiagramEditor.tsx`, `src/components/DiagramCanvas/Canvas.tsx`, `src/types/diagrams.ts`, `src/stores/useDiagramEditorStore.ts`
- **Priority:** **P0** (Shape libraries, Grouping, Alignment tools), **P1** (Templates, Auto-layout, Layers), **P2** (Hand-drawn style)
- **Verdict:** Canvas editing works well with unique freehand feature, but missing shape libraries makes it impractical for professional diagrams - can't compete without pre-built shapes
- **Sources:** [Excalidraw Features 2024](https://plus.excalidraw.com/blog/excalidraw-in-2024), [Diagramming Software Comparison](https://zapier.com/blog/flowchart-diagramming-software/), [Lucidchart Features](https://www.lucidchart.com/pages)

### Time Tracking
🔶 **Time Tracking System** (Status: PARTIAL - Reclassified 2025-11-28)
- **Audit Date:** 2025-11-28
- **Competitors Analyzed:** Toggl Track, Clockify, Harvest
- **Current Features:**
  - ✅ Timer tab with start/stop/pause functionality
  - ✅ Manual time entry creation
  - ✅ Project assignment
  - ✅ Description field for entries
  - ✅ Time entry list with edit/delete
  - ✅ Calendar view of time entries (integrated with events)
  - ✅ Summary/reports tab (monthly time report)
  - ✅ Projects management UI
  - ✅ Time entries grouped by date
  - ✅ Day detail modal (click day to see entries)
  - ✅ "Add Time Entry" quick button
  - ✅ Duration calculations
  - ✅ Project color coding
  - ✅ Unified Schedule page with 5 tabs
- **Critical Gaps (Preventing 100%+ Parity):**
  1. ❌ **Billable Hours System** - No billable/non-billable marking
     - Toggl Track: ✅ Mark entries as billable
     - Clockify: ✅ Billable/non-billable toggle FREE
     - Harvest: ✅ Billable hours tracking with rates
     - **Impact:** Can't track what to invoice clients for - baseline feature for freelancers
  2. ❌ **Billable Rates** - No per-project or per-task hourly rates
     - Toggl Track: ✅ Set billable rates per project
     - Clockify: ✅ Hourly rates FREE
     - Harvest: ✅ Per-person, per-project rates
     - **Impact:** Manual calculation of billable amounts - can't generate invoices
  3. ❌ **Idle Detection** - Timer runs when away from keyboard
     - Toggl Track: ✅ Idle detection with prompts
     - Clockify: ✅ Idle detection FREE
     - Harvest: ✅ Auto-stop idle timer
     - **Impact:** Inaccurate time tracking, over-billing risk
  4. ❌ **Invoicing** - No invoice generation from tracked time
     - Harvest: ✅ Comprehensive invoicing + payment acceptance
     - Clockify: ✅ One-click invoice generation
     - Toggl Track: ✅ Basic invoicing
     - **Impact:** Must use separate tool for billing - major workflow gap
  5. ❌ **Automatic Time Tracking** - No app/website monitoring
     - Toggl Track: ✅ Automatic tracking (paid)
     - Clockify: ✅ Automatic tracking FREE
     - **Impact:** Must remember to start/stop timer manually
  6. ❌ **Advanced Reporting** - Only basic monthly summary
     - ALL 3 competitors: ✅ Detailed reports with filters, exports, charts
     - **Impact:** Can't analyze productivity patterns or project profitability
  7. ❌ **Project Profitability** - No cost vs. billable analysis
     - Toggl Track: ✅ Profitability tracking
     - Harvest: ✅ Budget vs. actual, project margins
     - **Impact:** Don't know if projects are profitable
  8. ❌ **Pomodoro Integration** - No focus timer mode
     - Toggl Track: ✅ Pomodoro timer mode
     - Clockify: ✅ Pomodoro integration
     - **Impact:** Can't use for focused work sessions
- **Our Advantages:**
  - ✅ Calendar view integration (nice UX vs competitors' separate calendar)
  - ✅ Unified with events (all-in-one schedule page)
  - ✅ Offline-first, local storage
  - ✅ FREE (Toggl charges for many features, $10-20/user/mo)
  - ✅ Privacy-focused (no time data sent to cloud)
- **Files:** `src/pages/TimeTracking.tsx`, `src/components/TimeTrackingTimer.tsx`, `src/components/TimeEntryList.tsx`, `src/components/TimeEntrySummary.tsx`
- **Priority:** **P0** (Billable hours, Rates, Idle detection), **P1** (Invoicing, Advanced reports, Automatic tracking), **P2** (Pomodoro, Project profitability)
- **Verdict:** Basic time tracking works, but missing ALL professional freelancer features - can't be used for client billing, no competitive advantage vs Clockify free tier
- **Sources:** [Toggl vs Clockify vs Harvest](https://toggl.com/blog/clockify-vs-harvest), [Time Tracking Essential Features](https://thedigitalprojectmanager.com/tools/time-tracking-software/), [Idle Detection Features](https://hubstaff.com/idle-time-tracking-software)

### 8. Database Widget Feasibility

### 9. Privacy-Focused Services Analysis

- Proton Docs - Collaboration with encryption
- Proton Calendar - Encrypted events
- Proton Lumo AI - Privacy-preserving AI

**Key Questions:**

**Priority:** LOW (future consideration)

### 11. Apple Ecosystem Deep Dive

## FOUNDATION & INFRASTRUCTURE

### Current Foundation (✅ Completed)
- **Tech Stack:** React 19, TypeScript 5.9, Vite 7, Zustand 5, Tailwind 3, IndexedDB
- **Architecture:** Local-first, zero-cloud by default, privacy-first
- **Data Layer:** IndexedDB with Zustand state management
- **Validation:** Zod schemas for data integrity
- **Error Handling:** Error boundaries, recovery UI
- **Import/Export:** .brain format, ICS import, CSV export (planned)
- **Performance:** 60KB bundle size (vs 2-4MB competitors)
- **Offline:** Full offline functionality

### Infrastructure Gaps
⬜ **End-to-End Encryption for Sync**
- **Status:** Planned 2026
- **Implementation:** WebCrypto API, user keys
- **Priority:** HIGH (privacy differentiator)

⬜ **Service Worker for Offline Automation**
- **Use Case:** Run automations without browser open
- **Examples:** Daily task creation, reminders
- **Implementation:** Service Worker + Background Sync API
- **Priority:** HIGH (for automation features)

⬜ **IndexedDB Query Optimization**
- **Current:** Basic IndexedDB usage
- **Needed:** Compound indexes, full-text search
- **Priority:** MEDIUM

⬜ **Plugin System**
- **Inspiration:** Obsidian plugins
- **Use Case:** User-created extensions
- **Implementation:** Sandboxed JavaScript API
- **Priority:** LOW (future)

⬜ **Theme System**
- **Current:** Single theme
- **Needed:** Multiple themes, user-created
- **Priority:** LOW

⬜ **Export Formats**
- **Current:** .brain backup
- **Needed:** JSON, Markdown, CSV, PDF
- **Priority:** MEDIUM

⬜ **Import From Competitors**
- **Planned:** Trello, Asana, Todoist
- **Status:** Some work started
- **Priority:** HIGH (acquisition funnel)

## MARKET POSITIONING

### Neuman Brain's Blue Ocean Opportunity
**No competitor combines:**

lines: +Based on research into Notion, Coda, Airtable, ClickUp, Monday.com - features that all-in-one platforms have that we're missing:
+1. **❌ Database Views** - We have forms/tables but no:
+   - **Impact:** Limited ways to visualize and interact with structured data
+   - Notion/Airtable: Link tasks to projects, contacts to companies
+   - **Impact:** Data is siloed, no cross-referencing between types
+3. **❌ Rollups** - Can't aggregate data from related items
+   - Notion: Sum/count/average values from linked records
+   - **Impact:** Static data only, no automation of calculations
+5. **❌ Advanced Sorting & Filtering** - Limited compared to competitors
+   - **Impact:** Can't create sophisticated views of data
+1. **❌ Visual Automation Builder** - Our automation is code/JSON only
+   - **Impact:** Only technical users can create automations
+2. **❌ Cross-Database Automations** - Can't trigger across features
+   - Example: Completing task → create time entry → update project status
+1. **❌ API / Webhooks** - No external integration capability
+   - Notion/Coda/Airtable: REST APIs for external tools
+   - **Impact:** Isolated system, can't connect to other tools
+2. **❌ Import/Export Beyond Backup** - Limited data portability
+   - Notion: Embed Google Docs, Figma, YouTube, etc.
+   - **Impact:** Must leave app to view external resources
+1. **❌ AI Writing Assistant** - No AI help with notes
+   - Notion AI: Continue writing, summarize, improve writing
+   - Notion: Summarize meeting notes, long documents
+3. **❌ AI Task/Event Generation** - Can't extract from text
+   - Notion: "Add event: Team meeting Friday 3pm" → creates event
+Based on research into user demands and competitor features:
+### Financial Tracking Widgets (HIGH DEMAND - Not Implemented)
+   - What went well / what to improve

# Master Implementation Status - Neuman Brain Dashboard
**Created:** 2025-11-26
**Purpose:** Comprehensive audit of ALL features across user notes, research docs, and competitive analysis
**Status:** Draft - Awaiting User Approval
**Source Documents:**
- `tasks/Neuman Brain User Notes 11252025.txt` (User testing feedback)
- `tasks/research-document.md` (26 feature categories researched)
- `tasks/research-document-expanded-initial-dig.md` (50+ competitor analysis)

---

## Table of Contents
1. [Implementation Status Legend](#implementation-status-legend)

5. [Research Needed](#research-needed)
6. [Foundation & Infrastructure](#foundation--infrastructure)

---

## Implementation Status Legend

- **COMPLETED (100%)** - Feature fully implemented with all desired functionality
- **PARTIAL (<100%)** - Feature exists but missing key capabilities or polish
- **NOT STARTED** - Feature identified but no implementation yet
- **RESEARCH NEEDED** - Requires investigation before implementation planning

---

## COMPLETED FEATURES (100% Parity)

### Dashboard & Widgets
✅ **Task Summary Widget** - Dynamically updates with Quick Add (Fixed 2025-11-25)
✅ **Upcoming Events Widget** - Height fixed, displays upcoming events (Fixed 2025-11-25)
✅ **Quick Add Task Button** - Color contrast fixed (Fixed 2025-11-25)
✅ **Widget Layout System** - Flexible dashboard widget grid

### Schedule & Calendar
✅ **ICS Calendar Import** - Date format fixed, now working (Fixed 2025-11-25)
✅ **Calendar "Today" Highlighting** - Current day clearly marked
✅ **Double-Click Day Behavior** - Opens DayDetailModal with events/tasks (Fixed 2025-11-25)
  - Shows all events for selected day
  - Shows all tasks due that day with navigation
  - "Add New Event" button
  - Goes straight to add if empty
✅ **Kanban Tasks on Calendar** - Tasks with due dates appear on calendar (Fixed 2025-11-25)
  - Blue styling to distinguish from events
  - Shows title, priority, status
  - Clickable in DayDetailModal
✅ **Monthly Calendar Unification** - Summary tab calendar matches Calendar tab style (Fixed 2025-11-26)
  - Added min-h-[80px] cells
  - Clickable days open detail modal
  - Shows time entries for selected day
✅ **Sidebar Navigation** - Expandable children for Schedule sections
✅ **Event Start Date Picker** - Events can be created for any date (Fixed 2025-11-26)
  - Editable start date field
  - Defaults to clicked calendar day
  - End date validation

### Notes
✅ **Folders Sidebar** - Button overlap fixed (Fixed 2025-11-25, Enhanced 2025-11-26)
  - Collapse button at right edge (no overlap)
  - Expand button when collapsed

### Tasks & Project Management
🔶 **Kanban Board** (Status: PARTIAL - Reclassified 2025-11-28)
- **Audit Date:** 2025-11-28
- **Competitors Analyzed:** Trello, Asana Board View, Monday.com Kanban View
- **Current Features:**
  - ✅ Drag-and-drop between columns (@dnd-kit with pointer + touch sensors)
  - ✅ Customizable columns (ColumnManager, 3-7 column view options)
  - ✅ Card numbers (KAN-#) with search support - **UNIQUE ADVANTAGE**
  - ✅ Priority levels (low/medium/high) with color coding
  - ✅ Tags with autocomplete and filtering
  - ✅ Start dates + due dates
  - ✅ Checklists with real-time persistence
  - ✅ Comments with timestamps and activity log
  - ✅ Subtasks
  - ✅ Board and List views with toggle
  - ✅ Search (by title, description, or card number)
  - ✅ Advanced filters (priority, tags) with collapsible panel
  - ✅ Archived tasks view
  - ✅ Quick Add modal (Cmd/Ctrl+K global shortcut)
  - ✅ Keyboard navigation (arrow keys, Enter, Delete, ? for help)
  - ✅ Undo/Redo with toast notifications - **UNIQUE ADVANTAGE**
  - ✅ Auto-save on blur
  - ✅ View mode persistence (localStorage)
  - ✅ Effort estimates (XS/S/M/L/XL)
  - ✅ Custom status support
  - ✅ Time estimates and tracking integration
  - ✅ Custom fields integration
  - ✅ Recurring tasks integration
  - ✅ Task dependencies (blocks/blocked-by relationships)
- **Critical Gaps (Preventing 100%+ Parity):**
  1. ❌ **Assignees/Members** - Cannot assign tasks to team members
     - Trello: ✅ Multiple assignees per card
     - Asana: ✅ Single assignee + followers
     - Monday.com: ✅ Multiple people per task
     - **Impact:** MAJOR blocker for team collaboration - baseline feature in ALL competitors
  2. ❌ **File Attachments** - Cannot attach files to cards
     - Trello: ✅ Drag-drop images/docs, cloud storage integration
     - Asana: ✅ File attachments with preview
     - Monday.com: ✅ File columns with covers (crop/fit modes)
     - **Impact:** Limits use case to simple text-only tasks - baseline in ALL competitors
  3. ❌ **WIP Limits** - Cannot set column task limits
     - Monday.com: ✅ Task limits per column with warnings
     - Trello: Available via Power-Up
     - **Impact:** Missing key Kanban methodology feature
  4. ❌ **Card Covers/Images** - No visual covers on cards
     - Trello: ✅ Image covers from attachments
     - Monday.com: ✅ File column covers (crop/fit modes)
     - **Impact:** Less visual, harder to scan board at a glance
  5. ⚠️ **Calendar View Integration** - Calendar exists but on separate page
     - Trello: Premium feature, integrated view
     - Asana: ✅ One-click view switch
     - Monday.com: ✅ Instant view toggle
     - **Impact:** Less convenient workflow, requires navigation
- **Our Unique Advantages:**
  - ✅ Card numbers (KAN-#) - Unique to us, professional tracking
  - ✅ Undo/Redo - Unique to us, prevents mistakes
  - ✅ Better keyboard shortcuts than competitors
  - ✅ Offline-first, local storage (privacy advantage)
  - ✅ No paywalls (Timeline/Gantt included for free)
  - ✅ Faster performance (60KB vs 2-4MB competitors)
- **Files:** `src/widgets/Kanban.tsx`, `src/widgets/Kanban/CardDetailPanel.tsx`, `src/widgets/Kanban/KanbanCard.tsx`, `src/widgets/Kanban/KanbanColumn.tsx`, `src/widgets/Kanban/ListView.tsx`
- **Priority:** **P0** (Assignees, File Attachments), **P1** (WIP Limits, Card Covers)
- **Verdict:** MVP-level Kanban with excellent offline/keyboard UX, but missing baseline team collaboration features
- **Sources:** [Trello Kanban Features](https://www.projectmanager.com/blog/trello-kanban-board), [Asana Board View](https://help.asana.com/s/article/board-view?language=en_US), [Monday.com Kanban](https://support.monday.com/hc/en-us/articles/360000661379-The-Kanban-View)

🔶 **Timeline/Gantt View** (Status: PARTIAL - Reclassified 2025-11-28)
- **Audit Date:** 2025-11-28
- **Competitors Analyzed:** Monday.com Gantt Chart, Asana Gantt View, Microsoft Project
- **Current Features:**
  - ✅ Task bars with start/due dates visualization
  - ✅ Drag bars to reschedule (updates startDate/dueDate in real-time)
  - ✅ Resize bars to change duration
  - ✅ 3 zoom levels (day/week/month) with responsive scale
  - ✅ Today marker with indicator dot and label
  - ✅ Swimlanes by task status (backlog/todo/inprogress/review/done) - **UNIQUE ADVANTAGE**
  - ✅ Priority-based color coding
  - ✅ Dependency arrows (SimpleTaskDependency with SVG overlay)
  - ✅ Row-based positioning for clarity
  - ✅ Responsive scrolling and empty state handling
  - ✅ Tab interface in Tasks page (Board/List/Timeline toggle)
- **Critical Gaps (Preventing 100%+ Parity):**
  1. ❌ **Auto-shift Dependent Tasks** - When parent task moves, children don't auto-adjust
     - Monday.com: ✅ "Shift all dependent tasks by however many days needed"
     - Asana: ✅ "Auto-scheduling" - blocked tasks automatically adjust dates
     - MS Project: ✅ Standard behavior, cascading date updates
     - **Impact:** Manual rescheduling required defeats purpose of dependencies - baseline in ALL competitors
  2. ❌ **Critical Path Analysis** - Cannot identify project bottlenecks
     - Monday.com: ✅ Critical path visualization with highlighting
     - Asana: ✅ Premium+ feature, shows longest sequence
     - MS Project: ✅ Core feature, red highlighting
     - **Impact:** Can't optimize project timeline or identify delays - professional PM baseline
  3. ❌ **Baseline Comparison** - Cannot track planned vs actual schedule
     - Monday.com: ✅ Baseline snapshot, green/red variance indicators
     - MS Project: ✅ Up to 10 baselines with variance tracking
     - **Impact:** No way to measure schedule variance or slippage - project management necessity
  4. ❌ **Progress Tracking (% Complete)** - No visual completion indicators on bars
     - Monday.com: ✅ Shows progress percentage within bars
     - Asana: ✅ Task percentage of completion display
     - MS Project: ✅ Progress bars within Gantt bars (filled portion)
     - **Impact:** Can't see task progress at a glance - baseline feature
  5. ❌ **Milestone Markers** - No way to mark key deadlines on timeline
     - Monday.com: ✅ Milestone tracking with diamond markers
     - Asana: ✅ Milestones as specialized tasks
     - MS Project: ✅ Diamond milestone markers on timeline
     - **Impact:** Can't highlight critical dates/deliverables - essential for project planning
  6. ❌ **Professional Dependency Types** - Only simple blocks/blocked-by
     - MS Project: ✅ 4 dependency types (FS/SS/FF/SF) + lag time
     - **Note:** Type definitions EXIST in `src/types/index.ts` (TaskDependency interface with DependencyType enum) but only SimpleTaskDependency is implemented
     - **Impact:** Can't model complex project relationships (parallel tasks, delayed starts)
  7. ❌ **Subitem Support on Timeline** - Subtasks don't appear on Gantt
     - Monday.com: ✅ Subitems visible on timeline
     - MS Project: ✅ Summary tasks with subtask rollup
     - **Impact:** Can't see task breakdown hierarchy visually
  8. ❌ **Export Timeline** - Cannot export Gantt to PDF/Excel/image
     - Monday.com: ✅ Export to Excel
     - MS Project: ✅ Export to PDF, Excel, images
     - **Impact:** Can't share timeline with stakeholders outside app
- **Our Unique Advantages:**
  - ✅ Swimlanes by status (clearer organization than Asana)
  - ✅ Integrated with Kanban in single page (seamless workflow)
  - ✅ Free (Asana Gantt is Premium+ only)
  - ✅ Offline-first (works without internet)
  - ✅ Lightweight (no heavy dependencies)
- **Files:** `src/components/GanttView/GanttTimeline.tsx`, `src/components/GanttView/TimelineScale.tsx`, `src/components/GanttView/TaskBar.tsx`, `src/components/GanttView/DependencyLine.tsx`
- **Priority:** **P0** (Auto-shift, Critical Path, Baseline), **P1** (Progress %, Milestones, Professional Dependencies), **P2** (Export, Subitem support)
- **Verdict:** MVP-level Gantt with unique swimlane visualization, but missing professional project management capabilities
- **Sources:** [Monday.com Gantt Chart](https://support.monday.com/hc/en-us/articles/360015643840-The-Gantt-Chart-View-and-Widget), [Asana Gantt](https://help.asana.com/s/article/gantt-view?language=en_US), [MS Project Gantt](https://www.projectmanager.com/blog/microsoft-project-gantt-chart)

🔶 **Recurring Tasks** (Status: PARTIAL - Audited 2025-11-28)
- **Audit Date:** 2025-11-28
- **Competitors Analyzed:** Todoist, Asana, ClickUp
- **Current Features:**
  - ✅ Frequency patterns (daily, weekly, monthly, yearly) with intervals
  - ✅ Weekly: Days of week selection (M/T/W/Th/F/Sa/Su checkboxes)
  - ✅ Monthly: Day of month specification
  - ✅ End conditions: Never / After N occurrences / Until specific date
  - ✅ Auto-generation of next instance when current is completed
  - ✅ Next occurrence calculation and display
  - ✅ Parent-instance relationship tracking (recurrenceId, isRecurringParent)
  - ✅ RecurrencePicker UI component with all options
  - ✅ Integration with Kanban CardDetailPanel
  - ✅ Instance management (view all instances of recurring task)
- **Critical Gaps (Preventing 100%+ Parity):**
  1. ❌ **Recur from Completion Date** - Only recurs from original due date
     - Todoist: ✅ "every day" (from due) vs "every! day" (from completion)
     - ClickUp: ✅ "Recur from due date" vs "Recur from completion date" toggle
     - Asana: ✅ Same behavior with toggle
     - **Impact:** Flexible tasks (e.g., "call client every 5 days") can't be modeled - major use case gap
  2. ❌ **Natural Language Input** - No "every 2 weeks on Monday" parser
     - Todoist: ✅ Smart parser ("every weekday", "every 2 weeks", "every month on the 15th")
     - ClickUp: ✅ Natural language + visual picker hybrid
     - **Impact:** Slower to set up, requires clicking through UI - UX friction
  3. ❌ **Template Integration** - Recurring tasks don't pre-fill checklists/description
     - ClickUp: ✅ Recurring tasks can use templates (checklists, custom fields, etc.)
     - Asana: ✅ Task templates can be recurring
     - **Impact:** Repetitive setup work for complex recurring tasks (e.g., weekly report with same checklist)
  4. ⚠️ **Advanced Patterns** - No "last Friday of month" or "2nd Tuesday"
     - Todoist: ✅ "every last friday" pattern
     - **Impact:** Some business patterns can't be expressed (e.g., monthly all-hands meeting on first Monday)
- **Our Advantages:**
  - ✅ Full UI integration (not hidden in advanced settings)
  - ✅ Instance relationship tracking (can see all occurrences)
  - ✅ Free (Todoist advanced recurrence requires Premium)
  - ✅ Offline-first
- **Files:** `src/services/taskRecurrence.ts`, `src/components/RecurrencePicker.tsx`, `src/stores/useKanbanStore.ts` (lines 362-424)
- **Priority:** **P1** (Recur from completion - high user value), **P2** (Natural language, Template integration), **P3** (Advanced patterns)
- **Verdict:** Solid recurrence engine with all core patterns, but missing flexibility for real-world use cases
- **Sources:** [Todoist Recurring Tasks](https://todoist.com/help/articles/set-a-recurring-due-date-YUYVJJAV), [ClickUp Recurring Tasks](https://help.clickup.com/hc/en-us/articles/6310449699351-Recurring-Tasks), [Asana Recurring Tasks](https://help.asana.com/s/article/recurring-tasks?language=en_US)

       ```typescript
       export type DependencyType = 'finish-to-start' | 'start-to-start' | 'finish-to-finish' | 'start-to-finish';

       }
       ```

     - Asana: ✅ Basic dependencies only (similar to our current)
     - ClickUp: ✅ 4 types + waiting on/blocking
     - MS Project: ✅ All 4 types (FS/SS/FF/SF) + lag/lead time
     - **Impact:** Can't model parallel work (SS), coordinated finishes (FF), or complex schedules - professional PM baseline
  2. ❌ **Auto-shift Dependent Tasks** - Date changes don't cascade (already noted in Gantt audit)
     - ClickUp: ✅ "Rescheduling dependencies" option
     - MS Project: ✅ Automatic cascading by default
     - **Impact:** Manual rescheduling burden, defeats dependency purpose
  3. ❌ **Lag/Lead Time** - No "+3 days after" or "-2 days before"
     - MS Project: ✅ Lag time in dependency definition
     - **Impact:** Can't express "start QA 2 days before dev finishes" - real-world scheduling need
  4. ❌ **Dependency Warnings** - No alerts when blocker is overdue
     - ClickUp: ✅ Warning badges when blocked by overdue tasks
     - Monday.com: ✅ Dependency status indicators
     - **Impact:** Silent failures, tasks stuck without visibility
- **Quick Win Opportunity:** TaskDependency types already defined, just need to:
  1. Add type selector to CardDetailPanel dependency UI
  2. Update addDependency() to accept type + lag parameters
  3. Implement cascading date logic in GanttTimeline
  4. Add lag time input to dependency creation

- **Priority:** **P0** (Professional types + lag - types already defined!), **P1** (Auto-shift, Warnings)
- **Verdict:** Basic dependency tracking works, but professional PM features are defined but unimplemented - low-hanging fruit

### Notes & Knowledge Management
🔶 **Notes - Backlinks/Wikilinks** (Status: PARTIAL - Audited 2025-11-28)
- **Audit Date:** 2025-11-28
- **Competitors Analyzed:** Obsidian, Roam Research, Logseq
- **Current Implementation:**
  - ✅ Wiki-style link syntax: `[[Note Title]]` (LINK_PATTERN regex in backlinks.ts)
  - ✅ Link extraction from note content (`extractWikiLinks()`)
  - ✅ Link resolution: Exact + partial title matching (`resolveLinksToIds()`)
  - ✅ Backlinks panel component (BacklinksPanel.tsx) with count badge
  - ✅ Click backlink to navigate to source note
  - ✅ Empty state: "Use [[Note Title]] to create links" instruction
  - ✅ Auto-update linked notes when content changes (`updateLinkedNotes()`)
  - ✅ linkedNotes array stored on Note type
  - ✅ Backlinks panel integrated in NotesEditor
- **Critical Gaps (Preventing 100%+ Parity):**
  1. ❌ **Unlinked Mentions** - No detection of note titles mentioned without brackets
     - Obsidian: ✅ "Unlinked mentions" section showing implicit references
     - Roam: ✅ Detects unlinked references automatically
     - Logseq: ✅ Shows both linked and unlinked references
     - **Impact:** Miss implicit connections, manual linking burden
  2. ❌ **Link Autocomplete** - No `[[` trigger for note picker
     - Obsidian: ✅ Type `[[` to open note search popup
     - Roam: ✅ Instant search on `[[` with fuzzy matching
     - Logseq: ✅ Page autocomplete on `[[`
     - **Impact:** Must remember exact titles, typos create broken links
  3. ❌ **Broken Link Detection** - No warnings for links to non-existent notes
     - Obsidian: ✅ Purple color for broken links, "Create note" option
     - Roam: ✅ Auto-creates page on click
     - Logseq: ✅ Broken link styling + creation option
     - **Impact:** Silent failures, unclear if link is valid
  4. ❌ **Block-level Links** - Can't link to specific paragraphs/headings
     - Obsidian: ✅ `[[Note#Heading]]` and `[[Note^block-id]]` syntax
     - Roam: ✅ Block references with `((block-id))`
     - Logseq: ✅ Block embedding and references
     - **Impact:** Must link to entire note, not specific sections
  5. ❌ **Link Preview on Hover** - No popover showing linked note content
     - Obsidian: ✅ Hover preview with note excerpt
     - Roam: ✅ Inline preview on hover
     - **Impact:** Must click links to see content, slows research flow
- **Our Advantages:**
  - ✅ Partial matching (finds "JavaScript Basics" when linking "JavaScript")
  - ✅ Clean UI with count badges
  - ✅ Offline-first
- **Files:** `src/utils/backlinks.ts`, `src/components/BacklinksPanel.tsx`, `src/widgets/NotesEditor.tsx` (line 79), `src/stores/useNotesStore.ts`
- **Priority:** **P0** (Link autocomplete - UX blocker), **P1** (Unlinked mentions, Broken link detection), **P2** (Block links, Hover preview)
- **Verdict:** Basic bidirectional linking works, but missing PKM essentials for serious knowledge work
- **Sources:** [Obsidian Linking](https://help.obsidian.md/Linking+notes+and+files/Internal+links), [Roam Research Bidirectional Links](https://roamresearch.com/#/app/help/page/Roam_Research_White_Paper), [Logseq Backlinks](https://docs.logseq.com/#/page/contents)

🔶 **Notes - Graph View** (Status: PARTIAL - Audited 2025-11-28)
- **Audit Date:** 2025-11-28
- **Competitors Analyzed:** Obsidian, Roam Research, Logseq
- **Current Implementation:**
  - ✅ D3 force-directed graph (GraphCanvas.tsx using d3.forceSimulation)
  - ✅ Node types: Notes (circles) and Tags (differentiated)
  - ✅ Edge types: Backlinks (cyan arrows), Tag relationships
  - ✅ Zoom/pan with d3.zoom (scale 0.1-4x)
  - ✅ Drag nodes to reposition (with physics simulation)
  - ✅ Click node to navigate (onNodeClick callback)
  - ✅ Physics forces: collision, charge (repulsion), centering, link distance
  - ✅ Arrow markers for directed edges
  - ✅ Color coding: backlinks (cyan), regular edges (border color)
  - ✅ SVG-based rendering for performance
  - ✅ Graph data processing utilities (graphDataProcessor.ts)
  - ✅ Dedicated /graph-view page
- **Critical Gaps (Preventing 100%+ Parity):**
  1. ❌ **Local Graph** - No focused view for single note's connections
     - Obsidian: ✅ Local graph showing 1-2 hop neighbors
     - Roam: ✅ Page graph for current page
     - Logseq: ✅ Local graph with depth slider
     - **Impact:** Can't focus on specific note's neighborhood, overwhelming for large graphs
  2. ❌ **Filters** - No ability to filter by tags, folders, date ranges
     - Obsidian: ✅ Filter by tags, folders, paths, search terms
     - Logseq: ✅ Filter by page properties, tags, namespaces
     - **Impact:** Graph becomes unusable with 100+ notes, no way to focus
  3. ❌ **Node Sizing** - All nodes same size, no indication of importance
     - Obsidian: ✅ Node size = number of connections (degree centrality)
     - Roam: ✅ Node size based on reference count
     - **Impact:** Can't identify hub notes or important concepts visually
  4. ❌ **Link Strength** - No indication of connection frequency
     - Obsidian: ✅ Link thickness = number of references
     - **Impact:** All connections appear equal weight
  5. ❌ **Color Groups** - No automatic clustering or coloring by category
     - Obsidian: ✅ Color groups by tags, folders, or custom groups
     - Logseq: ✅ Namespace-based coloring
     - **Impact:** Hard to see thematic clusters in knowledge base
  6. ❌ **Orphan Detection** - No highlighting of isolated notes
     - Obsidian: ✅ Shows orphan notes (no connections)
     - **Impact:** Can't identify disconnected knowledge
  7. ❌ **Search in Graph** - No search to highlight specific nodes
     - Obsidian: ✅ Search bar highlights matching nodes
     - **Impact:** Must visually scan to find notes
- **Our Advantages:**
  - ✅ Tag relationships visualized (not just note-to-note)
  - ✅ Smooth physics simulation
  - ✅ Free (comparable to Obsidian which is also free for local graphs)
- **Files:** `src/components/Graph/GraphCanvas.tsx`, `src/utils/graphSimulation.ts`, `src/utils/graphDataProcessor.ts`, `src/pages/GraphView.tsx`
- **Priority:** **P0** (Local graph, Filters - unusable without these at scale), **P1** (Node sizing, Color groups), **P2** (Link strength, Search, Orphans)
- **Verdict:** Beautiful graph visualization, but impractical for actual knowledge management at scale
- **Sources:** [Obsidian Graph View](https://help.obsidian.md/Plugins/Graph+view), [Logseq Graph](https://docs.logseq.com/#/page/graph%20view), [Roam Graph](https://roamresearch.com/#/app/help/page/graph-view)

🔶 **Notes - Tags System** (Status: PARTIAL - Audited 2025-11-28)
- **Audit Date:** 2025-11-28
- **Competitors Analyzed:** Bear, Notion, Obsidian
- **Current Implementation:**
  - ✅ Array of string tags on Note type (`tags: string[]`)
  - ✅ TagPicker component with autocomplete (TagPicker.tsx)
  - ✅ Tag filtering in Notes page
  - ✅ Tag display on note cards
  - ✅ Free-text tag creation
  - ✅ Tags integrated with Graph View (tag nodes + relationships)
  - ✅ Daily notes auto-tags: `daily-note`, `{year}`, `month-{MM}`
- **Critical Gaps (Preventing 100%+ Parity):**
  1. ❌ **Nested Tags** - No hierarchy like `projects/web` or `archive/2023`
     - Bear: ✅ Multi-level tag hierarchy with `/` separator
     - Obsidian: ✅ Nested tags with `#parent/child` syntax
     - Notion: ✅ Tag groups with parent-child relationships
     - **Impact:** Flat tag namespace becomes messy with 50+ tags
  2. ❌ **Tag Manager** - No centralized tag management UI
     - Bear: ✅ Sidebar shows all tags with count and hierarchy
     - Notion: ✅ Tag database with rename/merge/delete
     - Obsidian: ✅ Tag pane with usage counts
     - **Impact:** Can't rename tags globally, typos create duplicates
  3. ❌ **Tag Colors** - No custom colors for visual organization
     - Bear: ✅ Custom tag colors/icons
     - Notion: ✅ Color-coded tags
     - **Impact:** All tags look the same, harder to scan
  4. ❌ **Tag Suggestions** - No smart tag recommendations based on content
     - Notion: ✅ AI-powered tag suggestions
     - **Impact:** Manual tagging burden, inconsistent taxonomy
  5. ❌ **Multi-tag Operations** - Can't bulk add/remove tags
     - Bear: ✅ Select multiple notes → add/remove tags
     - Notion: ✅ Bulk property editing
     - **Impact:** Tedious to reorganize or reclassify notes
  6. ❌ **Inline Tags** - Tags only in metadata, not in note body
     - Obsidian: ✅ `#tag` anywhere in note content
     - Roam: ✅ `#tag` and `#[[multi-word tag]]` inline
     - **Impact:** Must use separate UI, interrupts writing flow
- **Our Implementation:**
  - ✅ Works with autocomplete
  - ✅ Graph integration (unique)
  - ✅ Auto-tags for daily notes (helpful)
- **Files:** `src/types/notes.ts`, `src/components/TagPicker.tsx`, `src/components/TagFilter.tsx`, `src/stores/useNotesStore.ts`
- **Priority:** **P0** (Nested tags - essential for organization), **P1** (Tag Manager, Inline tags), **P2** (Colors, Suggestions, Bulk ops)
- **Verdict:** Basic tagging works, but missing organizational features for large note collections
- **Sources:** [Bear Tags](https://bear.app/faq/Tags%20&%20Linking/), [Notion Tags](https://www.notion.so/help/database-properties#select-and-multi-select), [Obsidian Tags](https://help.obsidian.md/Editing+and+formatting/Tags)
🔶 **Recurring Tasks** (Status: PARTIAL - Reclassified 2025-11-28)
- **Audit Date:** 2025-11-28
- **Competitors Analyzed:** Todoist, ClickUp, Asana
- **Current Features:**
  - ✅ Frequency patterns: daily/weekly/monthly/yearly
  - ✅ Custom intervals (every 2 weeks, every 3 months, etc.)
  - ✅ Days of week selection (for weekly recurrence)
  - ✅ Day of month selection (for monthly recurrence, 1-31)
  - ✅ End conditions: never/after N occurrences/until date
  - ✅ On-demand instance generation (not pre-generated)
  - ✅ Parent/instance relationship tracking (recurrenceId, isRecurringParent)
  - ✅ Next occurrence calculation
  - ✅ Instance deduplication (prevents duplicate instances)
  - ✅ Series end detection (respects endCount and endDate)
  - ✅ RecurrencePicker UI component integrated into CardDetailPanel
  - ✅ Visual recurrence editor (matches Calendar Events pattern)
  - ✅ Full RRULE-like pattern support
- **Critical Gaps (Preventing 100%+ Parity):**
  1. ❌ **Recur from Completion Date** - Tasks always recur from original date, not completion date
     - Todoist: ✅ "every" vs "every!" syntax - "every! 3 months" recurs 3 months from completion
     - Asana: ✅ "Periodically" option - repeats 1-30 days after task is completed
     - ClickUp: ✅ "Recur only after completion" checkbox
     - **Impact:** CRITICAL for maintenance tasks, habits, and periodic reviews where completion date matters
     - **Example:** "Change oil filter every 3 months" should be 3 months from LAST change, not original date
  2. ❌ **Natural Language Input** - No "every Monday" or "monthly" parsing
     - Todoist: ✅ Full natural language parsing ("every third Thursday", "monthly", "every 2 weeks on Friday")
     - ClickUp: Partial (date picker focused)
     - Asana: ❌ No natural language
     - **Impact:** Slower task creation, requires clicking through UI
  3. ❌ **Template Integration** - Recurring tasks don't use task templates
     - ClickUp: ✅ Recurring tasks can use task templates - each instance starts with same structure/checklists
     - Asana: ⚠️ Subtasks copy but not full templates
     - Todoist: ❌ No templates
     - **Impact:** Each recurring instance is identical copy - can't have dynamic checklists or varying structure
  4. ⚠️ **Recurrence Exceptions** - Can edit instances but no visual indication
     - Have `recurrenceException` field in types but not fully implemented in UI
     - No "edit just this instance" vs "edit all future instances" workflow
     - **Impact:** Confusing UX when instances are modified
- **Our Unique Advantages:**
  - ✅ On-demand generation (doesn't clutter task list with future instances)
  - ✅ Matches Calendar Events pattern (consistent UX)
  - ✅ Offline-first (works without internet)
  - ✅ Clean parent/instance relationship model
- **Files:** `src/services/taskRecurrence.ts` (lines 1-201), `src/utils/recurrence.ts`, `src/components/RecurrencePicker.tsx`, `src/types/index.ts` (lines 209-221)
- **Priority:** **P0** (Recur from completion), **P1** (Natural language, Template integration), **P2** (Exception handling UI)
- **Verdict:** Strong recurrence engine with professional patterns, but missing key flexibility (completion-based recurrence) that users expect
- **Sources:** [Todoist Recurring Tasks](https://www.todoist.com/help/articles/introduction-to-recurring-dates-YUYVJJAV), [ClickUp Recurring Tasks](https://help.clickup.com/hc/en-us/articles/6309885016471-Use-recurring-tasks), [Asana Recurring Tasks](https://blog.coupler.io/asana-recurring-tasks/)
